Comfortable and cute! I have not worn jeans for 5 years, but these are comfortable and cute. The reasons why are-1) I have Interstitial Cystitis which means I cannot endure any pressure on my pelvic floor, and 2) I gained a lot of weight during CoVid lockdown so nothing fit. I’m finally down a little. Currently I am 5’7”, 178pounds, and hips measure 45”. I think that makes me size 14-16 depending on fit. I got the XL. They are not at all tight, even a bit loose around my hips, but I have not washed them-just tried them on. These are the Classic Blue and they have good color, some contrasting lightening in strategic areas, and zero smell-which I only mention because other reviews had concerns about chemical scent. You could probably get away with a heeled sandal if wearing in late Spring, Summer, or early Fall. They will look like capris on tall people. I’m a regular 30” inseam and wouldn’t be able to wear any closed-toe shoes except flat tennis shoes (think converse low tops). Love that they’re machine washable, and I don’t think they’ll shrink too much. They seem to be pre washed. Hope that’s to you all!

True to size! First…I’m a leggings girl, I wear them everyday and have been searching for a pair of jeans (ug) that feel comfortable like my leggings do and I feel good wearing. These arrived next day after ordering. They are definitely NOT thin, they are a good weight, almost heavy and seem well made. They’re longer than I expected. I’m about 5’4, 160ish pounds, ordered a large and held my breath. The draw string is NOT just for adornment, it definitely tightens. When I tried them on I was excited they weren’t too small. The hem goes to the floor but I think with the slightest soles they’ll be perfect, and cute with my Vans Maryjane’s or my summer wedge sandals! Thankful for the elastic stretch around the waist, w/out that I think they would have been too tight. I have no idea what kind of top/blouse to wear with these fun jeans, so that will be an adventure figuring that out! lol…suggestions are welcome! Once I figure out how/what to wear with them, I’ll probably buy another pair!

Cute But the Pocket Lining is Visible & Bulky I ordered these in a size M and they are generally true to size. I'm 5'6", 28" waist, and a typical size 6/8 for reference. These looked like a very cute wide leg jean from the description pictures but the pocket inner lining can be seen from the front and it adds bulkiness around the top of the thigh where the pocket ends. Wish they would have made the pocket lining shorter and the pockets not so deep (you can see this in the picture). To be able to use these, I pushed the pocket liner up towards my waist and it isn't as apparent, but not perfect. These are a lightweight denim material, with a functioning drawstring waist and a wide leg opening. Overall, the jeans are truly high waisted, a good length, and a cute style!

Comfortable, true to size wide leg jeans Shopping for jeans is usually something that I prefer to do in person but lately it’s easier to risk buying online due to limited selection in most stores. This was a first and a great surprise. These jeans are really well made, they are true to size and are forgiving enough to weight fluctuations without excessive Spandex. They have a wide leg cut, mostly considered high waisted and have a “paper bag” waist with button closure and a bit of elastic on the back. There is a string to cinch the waist and emphasize the “paper bag design” but it is more of an accessory than a true functional part. Fabric has a nice weight and there are 6 color options, mine was Azure Mood which is like a light denim blue; overall they flow nicely without being too exaggerated. For reference, I’m 5’6” tall, curvy Latina who carry most of her weight on her lower half but still have a defined waist; size L fit me. There is a chance that I’ll remove the cord at the waist for a cleaner look or just change it for ribbon in a closer or even contrasting color. This style of jeans is a throw back to Y2K era, and can be styled with cropped tops, bodysuits, tops tucked half in/half out and may be your go to pair of jeans during Spring, Summer and milder days of Fall. If I have to be over critical of this product, I’ll agree with other customers who noted that tall people may have to proceed with caution, because they run a tad short. Overall 4.5 stars. Pros: • True to size, wide leg jeans • Fabric is soft, not too stretchy. Jeans come in 6 different shades. Cons: • Jeans may run a tad short, buyer beware.

I like I love a wide leg pant and these are cute. I am 5'5" and weigh approximately 150. I think I'm a pretty true size 12 and I ordered the large. The fit is true for me. My waist has not fully recovered from my last pregnancy and is thicker than what I'm used to, so there's a bit more snugness in the waist than I would prefer, but nothing unbearable. I consider these pants comfortable with the elastic give. I am not super leggy and this length is about perfect for me. I usually struggle with pants running under my heels in a regular length, and this length is absolutely perfect for me. So, if you are tall and leggy, plan on these being a cropped pant. Perhaps they were even meant to be a cropped pant, but they work as a full length for me. I would definitely order again.

They are soft but thick enough to be comfy and easy to wear. That said I am 5’6” and they are JUST long enough. These type of pants need to be just above foot length and I have to pull them down a bit for them to be like that. It’s fine, but if I were even a tiny bit taller they would be too short for me. So keep that in mind if you are in the taller side!

Comfortable and stretchy I love the stretch and comfort of these jeans. They’re form fitting but the material is soft and extremely flexible. They’re the jeggings with the 70s flare/bell bottom look. The material is thin however, so even though they have a lot of stretch, I can imagine they can easily wear down after so many machine washes. It sits high on the waist which can be firm flattering for curvier figures. I like that it has pockets that have a good amount of depth on both the sides and back. I ordered a size XS as I’m 5 ft 108lbs. It fits more like a small and the hem runs past my feet so I’ll likely have to tailor them or wear higher heels. $41 dollars is a bit steep for the thinness of the material but wearing comfortable jeans with this amount of stretch could be a flex.

My new go-to comfy pants The pants coming the best of yoga pants and jeans while having a cool bell bottom style. When I first took them out of the package I was afraid they would be way to small or tight. For reference, I ordered a size Medium and am 5'7", 142ish lbs. Once I put them on, I realized they have great stretch and comfort. The color is slightly lighter than I expected but I'm fine with it. They are thin for jeans, but not thin for yoga style pants. My only concern on recommending them to everyone is the length. I'm fairly tall and they are perfect so I would be concerned if a petite person ordered them as they may be too long? You can check the inseam length on the ad. I didn't find the size chart very on the ad so I just went with the size I usually wear. Good luck! They are super comfy. And I forgot to mention they have front and back pockets for your phone. 🙂

Way undersized! Inches smaller than standard sizing I ordered 2 pairs of white jeans in same exact size (28/6) on Prime day in October. This pair and one more expensive by Liverpool. I tried this pair on first and was beside myself. I gained a few pounds over the summer but what stunned I could barely get these jeans over my hips and forget buttoning them. Then I tried on Liverpool and those fit just fine even with my extra 6-7lbs of summer snacking gain so thankfully I’m still a size 6. Sometimes you literally get what you pay for. These pants are definitely more than 1 size too small than a 28 waist. Definitely size up by 1 size…maybe more frankly. Also the hems looked like someone took a hacksaw to them - unwearable and I would have had to trim them and distress them again myself if I kept them.

A must have in the every color! I own these cargo pants in just about every color, and I was shocked by the quality of the material. The fabric is smooth and gives the pants a tailored look, with an elastic band in the back that prevents gapping. There isn’t much stretch, but they are true to size. I typically wear a size 16 or 17 depending on the brand, and I purchased these in a size 18—they fit perfectly. The pants have functioning pockets and a real zipper, unlike some that just have decorative details. In my opinion, they are tall-friendly. At 5’6”, they look great with heels. These can easily be dressed up for an elevated casual look or paired with sneakers or flats and a tank top for a neat, casual style.

Must haves No joke the perfect pants. I had to write a review. The photos show freshly washed not steamed yet and you can tell the material doesn’t hold wrinkles so a steaming will make them smooth. They are quality made durable but still feel light and flowy. There’s a little sheen which makes them dressier than a regular khaki green pant. I’m 5’3 and for size 4 and will wear a platform or heel so they don’t need hemmed. The back is elastic band so no gap back there and they fit my waist and are loose on my bottom which is a bit hard to do in most pants. Four real pockets! 95% poly. 5% elastane so flowy, but a cotton-like feeling too. This pair is really quality made. The pocket pick is a better representation of the shade of olive green. Will machine wash well and steam to perfection!

Really cute and comfortable I was surprised how much I liked these jeans. I am 5'8" 125lbs and I ordered size 6. I went off the size chart and got the six because of the waist measurement. These fit well, but I probably could have gotten the size 4 for a more fitted look and still had a comfortable fit. The material is soft and stretchy, and also not too thin. These are straight wide leg and high waist. They also have a line detail down the front which is elongating and flattering. The length was ok, it hit right at my ankles, but I was kind of hoping they would be longer and slouchier like in the web pic. So, if you are above 5'8 they might hit short unless you are going for the ankle or cropped look. They have actually useable pockets on the front and back, as well. Overall, a really comfortable and cute pair of jeans, but pay attention to the length and you might want to size down.

Such a surprise! I’ve seen the trend of barrel jeans/pants and thought to my old millennial self that that trend would never be something I’d put in my wardrobe. But I saw these animal print barrel pants on someone at a theme park and thought they looked really cute on the older woman wearing them and so I figured I’d find me a pair and give it a shot. I’m 5’7” and weigh about 172lbs. I ordered a large in “brown” as I wasn’t sure what the other colors would look like. These pants fit great and honestly are a subtle barrel cut so they aren’t so in your face like some styles I’ve seen. I highly recommend these if you’re looking to try out the barrel jeans and want something subtle but still trendy!

SO WORTH IT!! I’ve always been obsessed with leopard print everything, so when I found out FP had leopard print barrel jeans I was stoked until I realized they were out of stock so I opted for these instead. I’m normally a M - L (145lbs 5’4”) and didn’t want to risk them being too small so I got the L and I can’t wear them without a belt. So if you’re between sizes, go with the size down. The button piece above the zipper does seem to be loosening but that’s fixable.

I bought these jeans for my kids. They like trendy clothes, they wear jeans with holes in them, and I thought these are a shoe in. The jeans arrived yesterday, and surprisingly my girls weren’t too interested. I think because it’s summer and they’re thinking shorts and mini skirts rather than jeans. Big win for me! I cannot believe how amazing I look in these jeans. Now, I’m not the type of person to compliment myself pretty much ever. I’ll be the first person to point out my wrinkles, jowls, saggy bat wing arms, and lumpy cellulite allll over. But these jeans make me look like a movie star. I don’t know what kind of magic was sewn into the fabric, but they’re comfy with just the right amount of stretch, they stay on all day… So they’re stretchy, but they snap back where they need to, they aren’t low rise, but they don’t go up to my chest. I’m telling you, these are perfect. I think the only people who might not love these jeans as much as I do are taller people. I say that only because I have a 30 inch inseam… a lot of my friends have a 32 or 33 inch inseam and these jeans would be a little bit too short for their liking. Some women prefer the ankle length look, and this would definitely work for that. These jeans are absolutely perfect on me and I’m going to be looking at some of the other colors as soon as I’m done writing this review.

5ft 4, Petite Friendly! I Moonwalked down the hallway! Oh my word it’s a size 4~~!! It’s really difficult to find a size 4 in stores and in general jeans that I’m not walking on because I’m a petite. The hems are right at the top of my feet. I’m 5’4”, Waist 26, Hip 36, and 107lbs. The elephant in the room needs to be addressed—yes the “light blue” is a little darker than in seller’s pictures. These are super soft and stretchy with a feeling that a distant relative of it could have been a Jegging! Lol I usually wear size 5. Size 5 is too big around the waist but it’s the looseness that I like in the legs. Size 4; complete opposite; perfect around the waist so they’re not falling down but tighter than my *personal* preference in the legs. Tighter around the legs is easier for me to deal with than having to hike them up constantly! They are not as heavy as my other jeans and they are not as cold when putting on. I’m very, very happy with these jeans, I skipped and moonwalked down the hallway!!

Runs big These jeans are so cute! Initially when I ordered, I went with amazons suggestion (which is usually pretty spot on), and ordered a medium. They were huge, but I loved them so I reordered in a small. Honestly, after getting them and wearing them, I could have done an extra small. They are a mid-rise fit, super stretchy, and comfortable (like no joke ladies if you’re still dropping it like it’s hot, these are for you). They are the perfect cropped length (I am 5’7), the white is not see through (I’m wearing magenta underwear in the pics), and I am seriously obsessed with the flared bottom detail. I was really hoping to just pop these in the dryer for some shrink action, but the label specifically says no dryer so I’m rolling with the looser fit. These definitely look more high end than they are, and I’m probably going to order the Jean version because I like them so much. I definitely recommend, but would suggest sizing down. For reference I am pretty universally a size 3 or small in most pants.

Tall girls wanted Here I go again, ordering pants I know will not suit a vertically-challenged chick as myself (5’1”). Sometimes it works out considering how you wear it, yes? These jeans, size small in medium blue, were not the case. I had to wear them below my waist/belly button. Otherwise, they would be too loose, requiring a belt.There is some stretch to these pants so I could pull them on and off without unbuttoning. Not that it matters anyway since there is extra fabric everywhere. I think if the hemline didn’t flare, these jeans would have been ok on me. They remind me of another pair I ordered here on the Amazon. But those worked better being wide-legged.I’ll keep these for now and hope I grow five inches overnight. The denim is medium weight. The quality is comparable to Gap. In the meantime, I’ll pair them with anything but flats or flip flops. Fake it til you make it as the fashion world says it.

Good quality jeans! I wasn't sure about these until I got them. I typically like to buy higher end jeans but these are a great quality and good fit! I am 5'2 and about 140 pounds and I ordered the sized medium which fit well. They are meant to be cropped which is the new trend I guess. I am trying to get on board with these trends. I think where they fall on my right at the ankle line is probably a bit off since I need a medium at 5'2. But that is an issue for me, not for the jeans! Know your size and where you want the jeans to fall. Otherwise, the material is lovely- it is softer and has a little stretch which is nice compared to most other jeans. I would recommend buying these!

Fit well and comfortable The picture shows one of many ways I plan on wearing these comfy pants. They have just the right amount of stretch. I washed mine on cold/delicates and hung them dry. Stretchy and Comfy I bought these to wear for work. I was touring a new cruise ship and needed something stylish but easy to move around in. These hit the spot. I do agree with others that the waistline is tapered but, for me, they worked ok. I have an hourglass figure so my body tapers at the waist naturally. If you have an apple shape, you will need to size up. I’m 5’4”, 160 pounds and the medium fit well. If I weighed a smidge over 160, they wouldn’t work. The fabric is soft and wasn’t see through with my skin tone underwear. The pockets sit high on the front and while functional, are a little awkwardly placed for truly holding items. There are pockets on the back that work great for normal use. This will be a staple in my wardrobe. I really enjoyed wearing them.

MOST AMAZING FIT EVER! WHAT ARE YOU WAITING FOR? GO BUY THIS JACKET!! I have not been paid or given a free product for this review. I just have a hard time finding clothes that fit good so I’m super excited about this purchase lol First thing I look for.. is this person shaped like me? So here it goes.. I ordered a large. I’m 5’1, 165lbs, 38DD or 36DDD, I guess I’m considered an hourglass shape ? My bust and hips are pretty much the same width and my waist is a few inches smaller. I carry my weight pretty evenly. Typically, I love how jean jackets look and hate how they fit so I was really skeptical and was pretty sure it would be returned but now I wish I could afford one in every color but I’m a single parent with 3 kids so mom splurges are limited but seriously... this jacket is amazing!!! So, let’s dive in to this review... THE MATERIAL AND FIT I’m the past, my experience with jean jackets has been awful. The material is thick, stiff, heavy, unyielding.. just not comfortable at all and having a large bust with a stiff jacket is seriously unflattering. If it fit my boobs, then it hung like a box at my waist. No definition at all. Think potato sack/tent... you get the picture 😂 NOT THIS JACKET!! This jacket has spandex or something in it! Not only does it fit my bust line but it curves in towards my waist beneath it. It is PERFECT and the material is a soft, thinner denim, but not thin like a shirt, it’s thick enough to function as a jacket and still keep you warm but soft and thin enough that is shapes to you and hugs your body... think, your favorite pair of jeans. That is how this jacket feels! I purchased a large, I’m a crazy in between size. I wear a 12 pants in women’s, 13/15 pants in juniors and my tops range from M-L-XL depending on the cut, brand, where it’s made. My waist screams “medium“ but my bust yells back “large” and if it’s not US sized... my whole body starts begging, “for the love of God woman get the XL... and don’t be scared of the 2X if it’s coming from China!” The large in this jacket was perfect. An XL would have been to big and a medium to open in the front, but I will be able to look great in this even if I lose 10-15 pounds (which I’m currently working on) I was diagnosed with Lupus two years ago and the medications caused me to gain a ton of weight so I’m happily 60lbs lighter since this past November and finally starting to see my body again! The fit of this jacket is AMAZING! It hangs with perfect spacing in the front if it’s unbuttoned. It stretches enough that I can button it up if I want, might be a tiny snug over the bust but the stretch is fantastic... and let’s face it, I’m not really going to button this thing. Does anyone really button up a denim jacket? I noticed some reviewers saying the arms were long and there was no pocket space. I didn’t have an issue with either and I’m short y’all! With my arms down, the cuff comes to the top of my fingers and if I raise my arm straight up over my head, it falls at the perfect wrist spot for the sleeves being down (but y’all know I’m going to cuff them anyway, I mean come on... it’s cuter that way lol) I also found there to be sufficient room in the pocket. My hand is completely open and stretched in the photo and fits. You could easily put your keys, gum or hands in the pocket... cell phone? No, but that’s not the jackets fault. Cell phones are ridiculously big and barely fit in our BACK pocket these days. Overall, my review is a YES, YES, YES.. I’ll have what she’s having in every single color... at least, as soon as my pocket book says I can lol !! (Little Harry Met Sally reference for you there 😉 yeah... I’m that old lol) So stop guessing, stop staring at it, stop debating... GO BUY THIS JACKET!! You will 💕💕💕💕 it!

Silver? Not so much, but still a great jacket! I really like the fit of this jacket. I'm taller (5'11") and it's hard to find sleeves that are long enough. These are just right, maybe slightly short when my arms aren't at my sides, but it's acceptable, especially since I tend to roll the sleeves on my jean jackets. It has a nice fitted waist area, though because I'm tall the slight flair at the bottom does hit my slightly above my hips. I don't anticipate buttoning the jacket often though so again, it's acceptable. Now, the color. I had read reviews so I knew what to expect but this is definitely a tan, bordering on khaki color. When I read silver, I think grey. Nope. Knowing it would be more beige than grey I still ordered it because I wanted to be able to wear it with all of my jeans (white jeans too). If you're hoping for grey, don't bother. I took a pic with two sweaters I have in my closet to show the grey vs beige, I also took a pic compared to some white jeans to show the difference there. I already have a dark blue jean jacket but it's pretty old with more of a cropped style...I might end up replacing it with a dark blue one of this style since I do like it so much.

Size up if you’re curvy I haven't worn jeans in ten years. I had these in my cart for months and rly happy I decided to finally get them! They fit true to size. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 175lbs. 32 waist, 45 hips. Usually I’m a size 12/14, but I ordered these in 16 for a looser fit and glad I did! The front pleats are well placed for a flattering fit, especially if you’re curvier. The material is fairly thick, but also stretchy. Surprisingly comfortable! They hold up well in the wash & don’t shrink when dried. The rise hits at just the right spot — although the waist is a little baggy on me, so I have to wear a belt. Very pleased with the quality. I never spend more than $20 on pants, but these are worth the money for sure. 10/10 recommend. If you’re questioning getting them, do itttt:)

Stretchy & comfy BUT diff colors have a different fit - check the material blend before ordering For reference, I'm 5'6" and ~135lbs with an athletic build. I got the pink in medium which fit perfectly. I wouldn't say this a true denim though, it's more like a light and incredibly stretchy cotton. Honestly, I wasn't expecting to like it as all the other denim jackets I've tried have felt too restrictive with little stretch (I have broad shoulders) but this one is incredibly stretchy and extremely comfortable, that I was quite surprised. It is a little boxy though (no shaping darts), but I don't plan to wear it closed, so it doesn't matter. I did find it more comfortable to roll the sleeves since they were a tad long, but it added to the cute style of this jacket . I live in a warmer climates and this is a perfect for my needs (cooler nights or air conditioned offices). I've worn it a few times already and it is starting to show a little wrinkling. I haven't washed it yet to know how it'll hold up. But I am already thinking about buying another color! Things to remember, it's lightweight, and very stretchy (at least the pink is) and treat it more as a layering piece or as an ensemble/accessory piece rather than a true 'coat' or jacket. If you live somewhere with 4 seasons, it'll be best for Spring or early Fall.Update 1-4-23: I loved the pink one so much, I bought the Always Black in the same size. Oddly enough, the Always Black one was too small, scratchy and stiff - no stretch at all. Looking closer at the material for each color I noticed they're different. For instance, most are a blend of 71% cotton, 27% polyester and 2% spandex. But the Always Black is 97% cotton and 3% poly, without any spandex. So that made a huge difference. So be warned, depending on color and fabric, it may fit and feel different!

Mine came w/HOLES + they fit LOOSE on me They are very stretchy with lots of room for progressing pregnancy, I guess. I am 5'3" / 120 lbs pre-pregnancy and usually wear size small. They hit at the ankle on me and are a too little loose to be a true "skinny" jean. I like my skinny jeans to look more like leggings. You can see how loose they look in my webcam pics. For temporary pants to get you through the 6 months you need something to wear, they seem okaaay. On the pair I received, I noticed at least 2 holes (1 large, 1 small) in the back, on the stretchy part near the hip & above where it meets the jeans. This was straight out of the package; I have not washed them yet. So I guess I lost the quality lottery with my pair. I was desperate for pants though so I am planning on repairing them myself with a needle/thread. If the rips were in the front, I might be less lazy about returning them. It's been difficult for me to find maternity pants that are small enough, but somehow these aren't as loose as others. It's still pretty early in my pregnancy (not quite halfway yet) so I don't expect the belly to fit just yet, but they are fairly loose around my thighs/hips so I feel I need to constantly pull them up. Not the most comfortable, but better than no pants. I'm hoping I like them better later in my pregnancy when my belly can help to hold them up. If after washing they are still quite loose, I'll just take them in a bit along the leg seams so I don't feel so frumpy.

Perfect!! Will buy more! These are amazing!!!! I will probably buy more colors!! I’m 5’7, 126-130lb normally and now 14 weeks along and I bought XS. I Just hit the stage where all my jeans are uncomfortable to wear around the waist, especially in the evening. I honestly didn’t have high expectations for these but they’re almost perfect. They fit snug around my legs but the material is stretchy and lightweight so comfortable. I’m skinny with thin legs so I don’t think I’ll gain a lot of weight in my legs. The waist band does start very low in the front so I will definitely have to wear my longer shirts with it but since the color of jean is dark I won’t really worry about the band showing. The rise is higher in the back. Love that it has real pockets in the back. They look like jeans I already own and love, I don’t think anyone will notice they’re maternity jeans.

Fit great I’m normally a 4/6 prepregnancy (5’8, 133) and at 21 weeks I ordered a small. Snug but not too tight. They fit perfect. I can tell they aren’t going to fall down during the day. The belly band has room to grow and can even fold over nicely to sit below my belly button (I hate things on my stomach but have a hard time finding below the belly bands) Im not sure what material the band is but it’s not thick or shiny and doesn’t have a tight rim at the top. Not sure how they will fit by 9 months, if it was winter I probably would try the next size too just to be sure the small were the best choice

UPDATE: 21 weeks & S still fits great. Belly panel slouches & has a run in it which is unfortunate. Still love them in every other way. Perfect maternity jeans! I’m 5’6” 125lbs pre pregnancy & usually wear a 2/4. I’m 16 weeks along & 135lbs right now & a small fits like a glove. I may order another pair in a medium for later in pregnancy if these get too tight. They are pretty stretchy so I may be able to keep wearing them for a while if not the whole time. Length is perfect & calves are not too tight to make it hard to get them on & off. I like that the top panel has a little room left for growth & sits high so I don’t have a line across the middle of my belly when I wear them.

Very cute! I am 5'4, 135 lbs and only 12 weeks and the regular mediums were the perfect length but just slightly snug on me in the hip/thigh area. I kind of wanted to trade them for the large but really didn't want baggy pants so I decided to stick with the mediums for now and will order the exact same pair in large if I gain anymore weight when I am further along. They are also very cute for maternity jeans, I really love the way they look.

If you’re small framed and are pretty much all belly..buy them. So I am currently 25 weeks pregnant weighing 128lbs, pre pregnancy I was 115lbs, wearing size small or 0-2. My pants started becoming snug pretty early because I DO NOT like my pants saggy anywhere. **SIDE NOTE - I’m ALL belly as of right now. I’ve tried numerous brands of maternity pants both in size 2 and size small and they were all SO big in the legs, butt and crotch area (not cute) I ended up ordering these as my last resort before I gave up, hoping and praying an XS would fit..and IT DID! Literally no sagging anywhere..so relieved I finally found pants that I don’t feel comfortable and are actually cute.

Cute skinny jeans after you can’t button your old ones I’m 20 wks pregnant and out of the 9 pairs of maternity pants I’ve tried so far, these have been the best fit. Probably later on I’ll want the full-panel belly, but for now these are a great transition from my regular pants. I got a medium based on reviews but had to return for a large. I’m 5’7” 150lb for reference. I’m a powerlifter so it was hard to find pants that fit my quads. I like that these are still cute and skinny and you can’t really tell that they’re maternity. They probably run a little short for a thin person but since I had to size up they’re the right length.

Great comfortable pants, review for the taller mamas I’m 5’8, 195lbs, 6 months pregnant. I finally had to admit to myself I need maternity clothes. These jeans are the first pants that I’ve tried that fit and are comfortable. I ordered a large. The jean waistband falls right below my actual belly and is comfortable. The black material that’s connected to it fits as long as I pull it up all the way up higher under my breast area. They are perfect for now but in a month or so I plan on buying an XL to accommodate my even larger size belly size. They are very comfortable and I don’t feel frumpy in them.

Not Quite What I Expected Capri-length is tough for petites, especially those of us on the shorter end of that spectrum, and these fall at a somewhat awkward spot on me at 5’ tall. I think I could get away with it if it didn’t bell from the knee down. It doesn’t appear to flare that much in the product photo, so they almost seem like different jeans … I understand the model’s legs are slimmer but the cut should be the same. Bring so wide right below my calf isn’t flattering (looks even wider in real life than in this photo). I usually wear between a size 6 and 8 and ordered these in an 8 and they fit but are pretty snug in my waist/belly. If you’re between sizes or find that you need more room in that area, I would recommend sizing up. There is definitely a lot of stretch, however, and I felt sucked in. The fabric is quite nice. It’s a quality pair of jeans, but if you’re petite, they may not be the jeans for you.

A Well Made Reversible Leather Belt... For Me I'm currently replacing old belts in my wardrobe accessories, and I really like reversible ones because I like the two color options in one belt. So, getting this AWAYTR Reversible Leather Belt was an easy decision for me. The first thing for me to say is that before I ordered it, I noticed that its design looked more like something I would wear instead of something my wife would, even though it's advertised first as a woman's belt. Once I was able to look at it in person, the style's definitely something I could wear. With that observation out of the way, here is my assessment of the belt. It is well made with good thick leather, a stylish belt buckle, and the stitching is really good. Reversing the belt is easy to do, and it locks solidly in place. The length of the belt can be cut down for more of a proper fit, if necessary. I found this info on the product page (Instructions for doing this did not come with the belt). I cut about 2 1/2" from the length to give me the correct fit for my 34" waist. Also, the two colors, black/coffee, are exactly the same as belts that I'm getting rid of, so I am really satisfied with this new belt.

Nice cargo pants I appreciate reviews when people give their body stats and and the size of the garment they ordered. It makes it so much easier to figure out what size you should order. So I am 5' 1 1/2" tall and weight is 156-lbs. and I ordered a large which fits perfect. I am very short waisted and these sit just slightly below the belly button on me. So I don't know that I would consider these high rise. The fabric has a tiny bit of stretch/give to it but its definitely what I would call a cotton twill. The color is a nice Army Green, lots of nice deep pockets and very comfortable. The washing instructions say to hang dry, but I will probably put them in the dryer on the lowest setting just to fluff. Also my inseam is about 30" and the length is perfect, if you have longer legs these would be too short. I hope this helps.

Pleasantly Surprised I had zero expectations for these, but I had them recommended by an Amazon influencer and decided to give them a try! First of all, I sized UP. I wanted a super baggy look, so after reading reviews I went with a Large instead of my usual Small. I’m glad I did! They’re super high rise, baggy in the legs but tighter in the waist. However, I can still pull them down lower and wear them slouchy. There is no stretch in the fabric, FYI. Length is long on me at 5’2” but I kinda like it. Print and fabric quality is actually really nice. But please note: they smell AWFUL right out of the bag. Like, truly so bad. I had to air them out overnight in my garage, then wash them inside out with detergent and laundry sanitizer. That seems to have helped tremendously. That’s really my only complaint. Still keeping them! :)

Nice addition to clothes! These belts are made of soft PU leather, making them fairly standard. They're suitable for keeping your clothing in place or adding a touch of style. I appreciate the pale gold hardware, which is a nice change from the usual silver. While I prefer rose gold, this color is acceptable. I'm grateful that this brand offers an extra small size. Since I often wear high-waisted bottoms, I need a smaller belt for my waist rather than my hips. For reference, I wear US size 0/00 or XS/XXS, with a waist measurement of 24-26 inches and hips measuring 32-34 inches. The price tag for these are around $10 for me right now, which I think is a great value for your wardrobe.

Great quality, inaccurate sizing. This is a stylish belt that is very well made. The buckle is secure and the material feels lovely. The loop itself makes this an item that is easy to dress up with empire waist dresses and outfits. I love this belt with the exception of the sizing being a little off. I ordered this in a large based on the provided size chart of my waist measuring at 33”. The belt buckles on the first hole provided but I could go in another notch if it was available to me. This leaves almost 10” of extra belt hanging at the end with no additional belt loop to keep it from sagging. The large belt measures 44” overall from buckle to end of belt tail. I am 5’2”, 140lbs with a 33” waist and size 8 pants, size small dresses. I could have easily done the medium and maybe even the small given the extra 10” of belt left. Overall. It is an absolutely lovely belt, high quality, comfortable and very stylish but if you are on the fence about sizing, size down.

Not As Described! I had ordered the navy pair of these leggings in M/L a week ago & absolutely loved them! They fit perfectly & are so warm & cozy. So I decided to order another pair in the skin color & they are COMPLETELY different! Different style, thickness & sizing. The skin has seams down the inside of the legs. Navy are seamless. Skin has 5" waistband, navy is 3". Skin is 8% spandex, navy is 15%. Skin has no front or back seam going up the front & back. Skin has regular leg openings at ankles, navy has sort of a stirrup. The skin pair did still fit me (5'3", 130lbs), but with less stretch than the navy pair. The fleece inside the skin pair is much thinner & not plush like the navy ones. However, the shade of this skin pair is a much better shade of "skin" than the shade shown on the website. Because I liked the shade so much I contemplated keeping them (they actually match my skin), but they were too thin to wear as pants & although they fit more like tights, the seams running down the inside of the legs made them unacceptable to me to wear as tights with my sweater dress. These had to go back & I reordered the skin colored ones from a different vendor. I'm hoping the other vendor actually sends the product advertised & doesn't pull a bait & switch as this vendor did.

Item is too big and too short at the same time, different composition than last ordered 2024 update: I lost my first and best fitting pair, so I only had the pair that were too short left. I learned my lesson, no more of these for me I bought these in 2020 or 2021 and LOVED them- I saw the reviews said to size up (I’m a small and 5’4 , so I ordered M/L) and they fit great, tall and just the right fit around the waist. I went back in my orders, and I RE ORDERED the same brand AND THE SAME SIZE, but once they arrived they are a completely different fit. Actually fairly skin toned! I've been looking for a good pair of "nude" colored fleece lined leggings since it's winter but I still want to wear dresses and skirts. I saw a lot of other stores on amazon selling the same style but with poor reviews, saying the coloring was very orange, so that was my main concern. I am very fair skinned (MAC NW10 foundation shade) and I didn't expect any to be very close but these actually are! Obviously they're not a perfect match as you can tell by the photos, but they're close enough to my skintone that it wont look weird. I can see these working well enough for many skintones from fair to medium. I got a size M/L just in case they ran small, I'm not 100% sure how big they stretch but they fit me well. I am 5'7", 130lbs, size 4-6. I would definitely recommend!

Comfy and warm I am very thin (5'5"/ 103 lbs) and the XS-S fits great. I will say that there seems to be no difference between back or front so I can't speak to how the fit would be on a fuller back side but they feel like they would stretch a little without pulling down. These rest perfect at my natural waist and don't slip down, there is no weird crotch fit and they aren't sheer for me. I love how they have the extra piece to go over the foot to keep them from riding up. The material is smooth and there is a sheen to them, my sweaters / shirts don't cling. The ankle does have a small band and for me there is a little bunching because I am small, but I always wear shoes or boots that go higher than the ankle. I am always cold and at 67 degrees in the house, I am warm. They fit under my jeans but it's snug, haven't tried with super skinny jeans and don't want to. They are definitely warmer than typical leggings if going out. If you're always cold, they are not miracle leggings - nothing is for me, lol! If I was a normal weight, I would try the next size up. They're nice, comfy, keep me warmer than usual, and I will buy more.

Comfortable and stylish! Here's my wife for this review:I wanted a pair of jeans that I could be comfortable in and look a little up to date with the trends and I didn't want them to be blue necessarily. I saw these offered in a few colors and loved the olive green color. I also loved that these are high waisted and have a loose fitting to these. The straight legged barrel look is popular right now and honestly it's a comfortable style to wear. I ordered them in a size 10 which is my go to size. I'm 5'6 and 160 pounds. I think they could be a little baggier but they fit me in the waist nicely. They're a LOT of stretch to these jeans which adds to the comfort. I can roll the pant legs up to create a wide hem which is in style today or I can leave them long which fits me like a good hemmed pair of jeans would be. I do have shorter legs than most people so perhaps this would be above the ankle on most people which is also the style today. I also love that you could just cut the hem and leave it raw which is also the style of today!I haven't worn them for a long time so I don't know how the stretch factor is. If I see an issue I will back but for now I really am enjoying these jeans. They're assembled nicely. I do see a little hesitation in the zipper when I zip them up, but I believe it's because of the stretch in the jeans. The zipper lays fine and doesn't gap or anything. For the money, style, and comfort, get yourself a pair of these.

Great if you're less endowed For reference I'm 5'4", 126 lbs, 29" waist, 37" hips with a smaller backside and I ordered these in size 6 according to their size chart, the solid green color. Lenth: at 5'4" these land just above the floor with no shoes on Waist: little loose, wonder if I should have sized down, however, these are supposed to be "baggy" so a little loose without feeling like they are falling down is fine with me, better than squeezing and creating a muffin top Look: definite differences with a side-by-side comparison (see pic.) Feel: slightly heavier/thicker than I imagined from stock photos. Soft and very comfortable 98% cotton, 2% spandex material, just feels good on. I actually really do like these jeans. Having a smaller backside, these are more flattering on me than other jeans. These are also very comfortable, I'd wear these if I had to catch a long flight, that comfortable. The extra decorative buttons do make extra jingle sounds. I hope this information helps with your purchasing decision.

Sidefeel Women's Denim Wide leg Capris High Waisted ...Gauchos These denim wide leg capri's used to be called Gauchos. They are high wasted, probably a bit too high for my frame, so I have a little excess material that is not filled out in the front. I chose the Light Blue denim. I am 5'4 or 5' 3.5" tall and approximately 120 or 122 lbs, I ordered size 8. I probably should have taken a size 6 but you can never tell with the cut of each brand. The material content is 70% cotton, 28.5 % polyester and 1.5% elastane. The denim feels like a good quality of denim (even though I prefer not to have polyester in my denim). The front pockets are functional and the back two (2) pockets are not. The front have a four (4) button closure. The length of the pants are 28 and 3/4 inches long. These nice denim wide leg capris/gaucho 's would be just fine all year round but of course in the winter you would wear either tights or socks with knee high boots. It is a recommend.

Okay, sometimes rips are meant as fashion but a single rip top of right leg does NOT look fashionable. Instead it makes these brand new jeans I just took out of the package this afternoon look worn and damaged. No time to return before travel and not convenient to do so due to travel plans. MY NEW FAVORITE PANTS!!!! Ok so I’ve been wanting some overalls for a while now and I finally took the leap! I had a pair from H&M a few years ago and I loved how they looked but I absolutely hated how they fit. They were super tight in the midsection and felt weird when I was sitting, so I was nervous to give overalls another try. But THESE ONES!? Oh my god. I usually change out of my jeans and into sweatpants the second I get home, but I wore these all night until I went to bed because they are just so freaking cozy. On top of that, they have tons and TONS of POCKETS!!! DEEP POCKETS LADIES, YOU HEARD THAT RIGHT!!!!!!! I’m talking about FIT YOUR ENTIRE PHONE in ‘em pockets!!! I could pass away. I usually wear a 6 in jeans so I got a medium and I will say they are a little loose, but I like that personally for me. If you want them more form fitting, I’d size down. But if you’re like me and you want a grunge-y 90’s look to layer sweaters under, these are for you! I have two cats who shed their light hair over EVERYTHING and I was a bit worried about getting black, but these were really to lint-roll the next day I wore them too. I’m also 5’8” and they fit really well in the legs so I imagine if you are any shorter you might find yourself cuffing the legs. Anyway, I have no complaints. Favorite comfy pants. I will be wearing these ALL WINTER!!!

A popular look - the wide-leg jean I normally prefer somewhat tighter jeans but have recently ordered several pairs that are wide-leg. These pants are just so much more comfortable than the skinnier jeans. I ordered a size Small - I am 5'5" and weigh about 107 lbs. When I ordered these pants, I thought that they would be a little shorter, maybe right above the ankle. However, these pants are regular pant length. The model in the picture is 5'8" and that's why the pants appear shorter in the picture. The pants are high waisted, so they sit right at your belly button. The pants have button closure and a zipper making the drawstring more of a decorative addition. The material feels pretty soft and is of good quality. My husband very much liked the way these pants looked on me. So, I can say that the pants are comfortable and look great - I will definitely wear them more frequently.

Good for a tall person, size runs small I was hopeful for these pants, but they aren't going to work for me unless I lose at least 20lbs. I am 5'7", 290lbs and generally wear XL-XXL comfortably, with the XXL being loose at times. I got a size XXL in these, and they are TIGHT. I won't wear them as is, as they are uncomfortably tight. You can see in the attached photos. Also, they are several inches longer than I thought they would be. Now, let's get to the good. The material is good quality. Not quite the old-school jean material, but definitely better than your usual nowadays jean material. They don't have much stretch--well, at least not enough to help with the fact that they run 1-2 sizes small. Now, I'm not used to buying "jeans" using a sizing scale of Small-XXL, but numbered sizes, but the sizing chart included stated XXL was 20-22, which is definitely not the case, as I fit comfortably into size 20 jeans that have some give, and 22s are generally a little roomy. The buttons and zipper are nice quality. The overall look, including color, is great. SO, as long you get the right size, these will be a nice addition to your wardrobe.

Great looks and perfect fit! I ordered the Black D pants, straight legs cut, in black, size M. I'm 5'7 and 134 lbs and it's a perfect fit! 🤩👍 After trying the same kind of pants that were more like constrictive (second-skin tight) jeggings rather than real pants, it's a great feel! These are more flattering. I like the jean style, the pockets and the zipper (tried other faux leather pants with only button fermeture and didn't like it) I read in other comments that the material is noisy and I have to agree with that, BUT nothing that a little mineral oil rub cannot fix. Besides, I am sure it won't be so obvious at a party. I also appreciate the material looks, not latex shine looking. I chose the straight legs cut for it's versatility; it can be worn with high heels as well as tucked in boots without the bulk. I didn't notice any smell. A little pricey in my opinion for faux leather (even with a discount) but I had been wanting those pants for a long while so... 🤷🏽‍♀️ I finally gave in, and I don't regret it! 😁 Also, sorry the photos are not the best quality, I need a better phone!

Well made but... Hey Amazon. This is an entirely unbiased and unpaid for product review. I got these for a cosplay I was doing (Pearl from Steven Universe). I originally meant to cut these into shorts however I kept them long because of weather. These came within a day with my student Prime which was excellent. The product was very well packaged and immediately I noticed how soft these were. They were the texture of a very thin velvet almost and the color stayed saturated even at my joints. Trying them on they fit all the way down to NY ankles. I'm 5'2" so I didnt really have any concerns, however, I believe a taller person would have problems with leg length because even for a short person these didn't go awfully long. Now although the material was very very soft it was also very thin. My butt was covered in my costume however I wouldn't recommend wearing these (at least the light colors) by themselves. Wearing standard underwear the pattern and crease of the underwear was very obvious and looked pretty bad. I think even with a thong it'd be way too obvious. So if you plan to wear these by themselves get a darker pair because the lighter pair won't satisfy your needs. With them being so thin they didn't keep in heat very well either and I was pretty cold even inside. Also, I developed a small hole near my crotch after dancing in them for a while, probably due to constant stretching. Overall I believe these are decent quality. I would consider buying a black pair as leggings if the material would be made a little thicker. If you need something for under a skirt or shorts then these are absolutely perfect. TDLR; Soft and well made but too thin

Nice leggings I ordered two pairs of the Plus Size: one in white and one in navy. I placed the order on Monday evening, and they arrived today, which was good not having to wait long to get them. I tried both on. They are very soft and stretchy, but fairly clingy on the thighs, behind, and mid-section (and I could see my cellulite with the white pair). I am 5'8" tall, weigh 180 pounds (yes, I'm overweight), and wear a size 14 or XL in bottoms. I don't see how these would fit as comfortably in sizes much larger than my size 14/XL if you're curvy and/or overweight. I liked that they were long enough to reach ankles even after getting them around my hips, behind, and tummy. My ankles are small and lower legs are thin, and these were not way too loose at the bottom. I included a picture my thigh in the white pair with a red paper underneath to show that the white is somewhat sheer, and I/you can see through them some. But that's okay with me, since I'll only be wearing these with long, loose tops, so they won't be visible much above the thighs. The navy pair was not sheer to me. Overall, I like them, would recommend buying them, and will probably reorder in at least one other color. Hopefully, this long review was .

Old leggings are obsolete,look no further this is your NEW generation leggings ! Believe it or not this is my first try with this type of material in leggings, and I love it ! If you remember the old ones did not shape well and kind of left your figure looking odd shaped and very exposed so the best way we deal with it is " time to bring out the big tunic " and cover all that up. Well the material in these are magical , soft and lovely suedy feel they also SMOOTH things out very well and give nice shape to you. Now don't get me wrong I still need to cover my c section shelf belly which easily can be done with a nice loose short top. but that's it don't need to bring out the big (guns) :) tunics for this. You can once again show your great shape without covering everything up and NOT see thru ! One point Im adding is im 5'5 165lbs size 14 and brought plus size, the waist band is tight but the rest is loose not sure this will fit easily on anyone size 18 and up I just wish I could have tried the yoga waist band ones. Hope I helped you.

The softest leggings you will ever find... HOWEVER... I purchased these leggings to make an outfit with the new fad of Kimono tops. I selected the pair in white. First, let me say that when I opened the package of these and touched them for the first time, I totally fell in love. They were so amazingly soft. However, my love instantly turned to disdain after I discovered all of the issues with the leggings. First, the entire seam of the back of the leggings (at the buttocks) was half-ripped and looked as if there was an issue when sewing them. (See photos) I immediately knew that I had to return them but I wanted to get a new pair, so I did an exchange. The other issue with my first order was that it had strange stains in multiple places. I notified Amazon and got my exchange started. The new pair arrived quickly and they were just as soft and luxurious as the first leggings! Thankfully, this time the leggings were free of any defects. They fit perfectly (I am 5'8, 185 pounds and all hips and thighs- I purchased a Plus Size). I probably could have ordered the next size down, too.The white leggings are slightly see-through and be fair warned, if your legs aren't toned, it will look like you got hit in the legs by a bag of nickels. Regardless, I still liked them a ton and will likely buy them in other colors!

Fits nice, stretchy, dark dark black I ordered three different types of Levi’s trucker jackets all in size Large (recommended size) for a side by side comparison. Please note that the 3 trucker jackets DO NOT COME WITH A HOODIE. I was simply making sure I could layer a hoodie underneath the denim jackets as this is how I like to wear them. All 3 Large sized jackets passed my hoodie test and Large felt like an accurate size recommendation to the style of each jacket. The sleeve length was long and perfect for me. If you’re looking for a more “form fitting” jacket, order the original trucker in your normal size (it does have stretch if you’re between sizes), order the Ex-Boyfriend a size smaller (no stretch.. watch out for arm length though), and skip the Baggy trucker altogether or try ordering it two sizes smaller (no stretch.. watch out for arm length changes ordering small sizes). My measurements: Height 5’8” - Weight 155lbs - Shoulder to Shoulder 17" - Bust 38" - Waist 28” - Hips 40". All of the Levi’s denim trucker jackets have inside “pockets” which is a feature I have always loved from Levi’s denim jackets. If you notice from the photos, each jacket is about 2” longer than the next. The Original trucker jacket being the shortest length and darkest wash; pictured in the first photo, all the way to the left (top & bottom) of the second photo and all the way to the right in the third photo. It is meant to be roomy but more form fitting, while the other two styles are meant to be baggy. I was still able to layer the Original trucker over my hoodie comfortably in my recommended size. While the Original trucker fit nicely and probably would look best over dresses out of the 3, I chose the Ex-Boyfriend because I already own a form fitting denim jacket and wanted something different. I liked the Original trucker the least because it was so dark, had all black buttons, and looked too new and clean. This sounds weird, but all my black jeans are all faded, so I preferred the faded washes on the other two black trucker jackets. The Baggy Trucker and Ex-Boyfriend trucker also have no stretch and have contrasting brownish brass buttons which felt more vintage. Notice the Ex-Boyfriend jacket has buttons on the right “men’s side” while both the Original Trucker and Baggy Trucker have buttons on the left “women’s side”. I hope my review helps you decide!

A must have wardrobe staple! Love this jacket! Hits right at my hips and has good feel in weight but not too heavy; just perfect for transition from summer to autumn. The color is in between dark denim and light denim. It has a slight stretch that I noticed most in the lower area of the last button. I am a classic Apple shape, stand 4’11.5”, weigh 155 lbs. and purchased a Medium. I was able to button it all the way including area around my tummy which is where I carry most of my weight. It fit like a glove with just enough give that I didn’t feel I couldn’t breathe in it or that it was too tight. Inside it also looks like there are two rather large hidden pockets on left & right side. You could easily fit an iPhone inside - see the pics I included. I have bigger arms and found that the upper sleeves were very roomy & would fit great for those gals with larger arms. I love the slanted side pockets on the front. Don’t wait on this - purchase it already! You will not regret it and it’s Levi’s brand so probably will last a long time.

LEVI WINS AGAIN I decided to purchase a large based off of reviews and I'm happy I did although I believe a medium would have fit too. I am 5'8" and 150lbs and I am typically a true medium in clothing. I have broader shoulders than most females and my frame is your average mom build. It's the perfect medium wash jean color and I'm looking forward to wearing this all fall long!

Comfy; Large fits US size 10 These are not actually what I consider "jeggings". These are colored, fleece-lined leggings. I ordered the "navy blue- fleece-lined", and they could be mistaken for jeans from a distance, but not up close, since the material is usual leggings material in a blue color, with light stitching. They are very stretchy and comfortable, and pass the "squat test" (i.e. they are not see-through when I bend over). The back pockets are actual pockets, but the front ones are just for looks. As for sizing, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 165 pounds, have a short torso with a flat derriere and big stomach, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 x 32 (medium leggings and pants at Old Navy and H&M). I ordered these leggings in a large since fleece-lined leggings sometimes run smaller, and they fit perfectly without slipping down or digging into my waist. They do not offer much tummy control, and the back pockets are placed a little low for my preference. On me, they are full length and not 7/8 (see my pics). Overall, I am happy with the warmth and comfort of these leggings, which I plan to wear all winter with long sweaters. I would definitely order them again.

Size up if you are going to machine dry I like the style of these mid rise jeans and they are flattering on my hourglass figure. I usually wear a womens 10 in pants and ordered a large based on the size chart. The fit me perfectly when I tried them on; however, they shank when I washed them. I washed these jeans in cold water and tumble dried them on a low temperature setting (the tag recommends hand washing and line drying, but who has time for that?). The length is still ok on me after drying them. I'm 5'6" and the length is good to wear with sneakers or flats, but the jeans would probably be a little too short to wear with heels. After washing, the stomach area is now a little tight on me. I could still wear them if it wasn't for the fact that the light weight denim fabric pulls out of shape at the zipper. I know these are mid-rise jeans, but the rise feels a little short. With the light weight fabric and the rise, there is a very subtle camel toe situation going on for me. There must have been an adhesive label on the back side of the waistband that I didn't notice before I washed them because there is now a spot of gummed adhesive that will probably never come out. The style of the pants is cute and the quality is fine, though they seem a little over priced at $39.99. If they hadn't shrunk, I would have been happy with them. If you plan to tumble dry these and wash in any temperature other than cold I would recommend sizing up at least a size.

Happiness Achieved: Soft, Comfortable Jeans 24 Short; Dimensions As Listed! I haven’t worn jeans in many years, but I thought I should have a pair as they are useful. Mostly I wander about in some form of loose lounge pants. I like that these are made of a stretchable fabric so are comfortable. They really are comfortable, and I'm very particular about that these days. The fabric yields but look like jeans. The stitching and the sewing is very well done and I don't see any flaws--the quality is exceptional. The zipper slides up and down just fine. The button hole is a little tight but that will loosen. I'm definitely going to use these. I ordered an extra large size "24 short" figuring there would be shrinkage and I like things loose. I’m 5’3” around 180. Probably I'm normally a size 18 to 20. But I really don't like feeling the constriction of thick materials. The legs have plenty of room. For my height I have to roll them up about an inch and a half (see photo; sorry, am by myself and don't use mirrors, so these shots are the best I can do.) Jeans size 24 Short: 45.5" waist, 55.5" hips, and 18" bottom of the leg circumference (leg opening) My measurements: 47.5"waist, 52" hip Five pockets: A front pocket will hold my phone fine; I may add Velcro to secure it. The back pockets are very large, good for gardening gloves and a trowel. I have a wide waist and the jeans' waist fits me well and is not tight or constrictive, being a loose larger size than I normally wear. There's lots of room, and I may even use a belt. Happy to find some quality plus-size clothing.

Happiness Achieved: Soft, Comfortable Jeans 24 Short; Dimensions As Listed! I haven’t worn jeans in many years, but I thought I should have a pair as they are useful. Mostly I wander about in some form of loose lounge pants. I like that these are made of a stretchable fabric so are comfortable. They really are comfortable, and I'm very particular about that these days. The fabric yields but look like jeans. The stitching and the sewing is very well done and I don't see any flaws--the quality is exceptional. The zipper slides up and down just fine. The button hole is a little tight but that will loosen. Waist isn't best for curvy girls I wanted to love these because the denim color was perfect, and the fleece inside felt super-soft. Unfortunately, although these weren't tight in the legs, they had trouble accommodating my butt. I had the dreaded giant gap in the back of the waist band which is very uncomfortable. In the front, these didn't even come up to my belly button even though they do on the model. The waist was just very weird for this size (XXL). You might be able to reign in the waist with a belt to gather up the extra fabric and eliminate the gap in the back. If you're tall, be aware that these will be high-waters on you. I'm 5'8" and would not be able to wear these without looking silly. These have a real zipper and button in case you were curious, they aren't a pull-on pant. These were a "no" for me based on how they fit my body, but construction-wise they are high quality. I didn't see any loose threads, crooked stitching, or anything else. If you wear a smaller size and are 5'6" or shorter, these might work better for you.

Not for long legs. I was so excited to get these insulated jeans, just in time for cooler weather! The quality is nice, but the fit just doesn't work for me. (5' 10" 155 lbs. 30" inseam wearing a size L) Pros: Cozy, soft insulated jeans that look like any other pair of jeans. Color and stone washing add cute styling. They do fit fairly true to size, just not quite right for me. They feel like quality materials. Cons: They are too short! To be fair, most standard jeans are, but these feel extra short. They bunch funny around the knee. The fit at my waist, hips, bum looks like riding pants...too loose in the wrong places. I'm keeping the pants, I don't hate them, I will just wear high boots and long sweaters, sweatshirts or jackets to hide the akward parts and enjoy the warmth of these soft insulated jeans.

Perfect fit-Extremely comfortable and warm! I'd give these 10 stars if I could. I'm 5'2", 114 lbs and the Small fits me perfectly, and I mean perfectly, even the length. I will not have to hem these at all, so they may be more cropped on someone that is much taller. That may not be an issue if you plan to wear these tucked into boots.I really like the look of the darker blue wash on these that have a little fading in the right places. The lining is soft and warm. Overall these jeans are extremely comfortable. The jean material is very soft, not stiff with the right amount of stretch which makes these fit so well. The button and zipper both work easily as expected. There is no gap on the back of the waist band that I encounter with some jeans. The stitching looks great and is uniform throughout. The front pockets are deep enough to use and not just for looks. The back pockets are lined with the same fleece as the inside of the jeans which was a nice surprise.I am very happy with every aspect of these and know these will get a lot of use this Fall and Winter.

Best warming jeans I own! Now that summer is over it’s already time to start bundling up. These jeans kept me warm all night in 40F weather! The medium fits very well around my waist and I usually wear a size 4 pants. The cons are the button and knees. The button seems very loose like it may pop off at some point in the suture. The knees are also on the looser side and I’m not a fan of the baggy-ness so I cuffed the bottom to try and make them look a little more fitting. These are very stretchy like jeggings which is a great plus because I could bend in them with no problem. And the softness inside is so much better than expected! It’s a very thin layer of fur rather than some scrappy fuzz inside like the old navy pair I own. The fabric itself is thick and good quality. They really do insulate and provide warmth! I could easily wear a pair of leggings under them for extra cold days. Overall I would recommend these to anyone looking for some warmth!

Curvy Chic Favorite Jeans - Not for bean-pole girls! STYLE: - The waist has an adorable 3-button detail that is super-flattering and holds in any pudgy tummy if you ate too many carbs that day ;) - Easy to wear and match with everything - we all need a good pair of white pants. - These pants make you FEEL confident & sexy which actually MAKES you confident & sexy! - I don't know what it is about white pants but men LOVE when I wear these - especially with heels!!! Seriously, I get stared at to the point of awkward, sometimes. lol SIZING: - I usually buy Wax jeans, jeggings & shorts and the sizing is pretty consistent. Also, my weight fluctuates so I like that they use stretchy, forgiving material that hugs in a flattering way. - I'm 5'8", 34D, 150 lbs, and measure 38/31/41 and I usually go with size 13 unless I want a super tight fit, then size 11. - I have pretty muscular calves (15.5" circumference) and these pants, even in the larger of the 2 sizes I order: 13 vs 11, still hug very tightly to the calves which causes creasing & gathering at the knees - see pics). - The length is otherwise perfect. - Designed for a snug fit from waist to ankle, so don't size down. They'll be tight enough unless you have zero muscles and/or fat. FABRIC: - The fabric shrinks temporarily after washed and then stretches as you wear it, so keep that in mind. - The pockets show through fabric (in white anyway) but my undies don't show when I wear tan or beige. - Thong looks best with these pants, as granny-panties don't let the fabric & back seam do it's form-fitting thing to show off your ass ;) COMFORT: - Soft, movable fabric make these a very comfortable all-day jean. - Waist is a bit loose on me, so I end up having to play tug-of-war with them if I don't wear a belt, especially if I wear them for several hours (because they stretch & relax as you wear them - as mentioned above). Overall opinion: As a curvy chic, these are by far the best fitting brand and I will keep buying Wax jeans as long as available! So I suggest: Buy them and do so in a few colors. Make sure white is one of them but also get some in colors you are confident to wear when you're on your cycle (in a larger size if needed). They look HOT with pumps, knee boots, flip-flops, strappy flats and even barefeet! Don't forget to click so I know!

NOT MIDRISE First problem: the description says "mid-rise", yet the picture is of jeans that are CLEARLY LOW RISE. Honestly I was looking for low rise, but decided I would settle for mid-rise if that's what I received. Ordered a size 7 first (5'5", 135 lbs) which was WAY too small, plus the tags on what they sent me all said "High-rise". Returned those and ordered size 9 thinking the high-rise part was just a fluke. Nope. They sent me high-rise again (as can be seen in the picture) and on top of that, they are slightly loose in the waist, which means they would be falling down in no time. Sending these back for a refund. Won't be ordering again.

Cute jeans, surprisingly a little too long I've ordered another wax denim in a size 11 that fit well but these were a little too tight, might be because the other pair had more stretch and these weren't very stretchy (for reference, I'm 5'8", 160 lbs, 36D, 32" waist, 40" hips). The waist cut into me and gave me a muffin top and the legs gave me serious "stuffed sausage" look below my butt. The fabric is thick enough so my underwear didn't show through and seemed good quality. I worry about jeans being too short but these were actually a bit long on me causing the fabric to gather around my ankles which I didn't really mind. I love the 3 button detail and white jeans are a staple item so if you can get the sizing right, I recommend buying them.

A break from high-waisted, casual wear, may be too long for some … Sidefeel Women Casual Loose Mid Rise Cargo Jeans GREAT I can see my belly button. I can breathe. Not high-waisted. They sit more-so on my hips. While I don’t really want to go back to all 90s styles, I enjoy getting away from crazy tight/too high-waisted pants when I can! POCKETS The front pockets fit my iPhone SE. It’s a little awkward when I first sit down (the phone digs into my hip a little), but it’s not like the phone is going to fall out. I think I will eventually not notice it. The back pockets also fit my iPhone SE, but when I sit down it sticks out quite a bit and I am not sure my phone will stay secure. OKAY The material is heavier than I expected, but it is not uncomfortable. Without shoes on these hit the top of my feet/down to the back of my heel. They almost drag on the floor – almost but not quite. So, the length is okay for me. I am 5’8’’. I don’t know if it will be an issue for others. SIZING If I followed their size chart based on my measurements only, I could have potentially gone with a size 4 which lists “hip 37.4”. However, I chose to follow the suggestions of previous reviewers to size up to a size 6 which lists “hip 38.39”. I am happy with my size choice. For reference, I am 5'8" and 125 lbs, with an athletic build, and my current measurements are bust 35.5, 28.5 inches for my waist, and 37.5 inches for my hips. OVERALL There is nothing necessarily wrong with the style of these pants, but there is nothing spectacular either. They are definitely for casual wear – not crazy flattering or unflattering for that matter. So, I don’t not recommend them. They are high quality and a break from the norm – you’ll have to decide for yourself on the style.

They are definitely for casual wear – not crazy flattering or unflattering for that matter. So, I don’t not recommend them. They are high quality and a break from the norm – you’ll have to decide for yourself on the style.

Great pants, just not right for me So let me start off by saying that the first impression quality is great on these. There were a few loose threads in the zipper area, but most of those were easily pulled free and its no big deal. Moving on from that, I got the size 18 in grey twill. I usually buy 16A jeans. These jeans are very long. I admit I didnt pay attention to the inseam length as that isn't usually a problem for me being about average height (5' 5 or so). These are like 5 or 6 inches too long. See my pic, my feet are flat. I dont normally wear heels so that is an issue. Next, I think the fabric might almost be too stretchy. Or perhaps just a little too thin. I have quite a bit of a pudge in front of my stomach. Most of my jeans, even the super stretchy skinny jeans, even it out. These don't. And its very obvious. Finally these fit super well around the waist but got a bit too loose around the knees. Not much an issue but there you are. As for a huge plus! There's front pockets!🎉🎉 Im not sure if I'll return yet as they're not bad pants, just not quite right for me which is a shame because they're such a good price.

Thick hips and booty approved I'm 5' 5" with a smaller bust/waist, but LARGE hips and a** (dictionary pear-shape), ON TOP of being plus sized. Other pants I've bought have a gap in the back, every time, without fail, sometimes as much as 3"-4" between my back and the pants themselves, solely due to needing the wideness of the larger sizes for my more curvaceous areas. Even with belts, it's a struggle. These pants do not have a gap. None. Flush against my body all the way around, even in the back. I was FLOORED. I am a US18W (while my tops run closer to a smaller US16/larger US14) just to scale just how much of a size difference there is when it comes to waist vs hips), if you're built like a pear you understand how much of a pain the gap fight is. There's just enough stretch that even at my widest points, they're comfortably snug. Bit of a problem area in my belly after losing weight, front of pants fit comfortably without pressing into/floating over my lower torso area. If you're unsure about buying pants online because you have curvy-girl problems, buy the pants. Just put them in your cart. I bought two pairs of this brand, I'll be replacing all my old jeans with additional pairs soon. The struggle is real, ladies, let these pants handle your curves like a boss.

Runs slightly large I am about 165 lbs @ 5'3, measure 36DD-31-41, and wear a sz 12 in most jeans. I bought these in a size 12. I like the overall feel and style of the pants, the length, and that the waistband doesn't pinch or cause love handles. However, they are slightly too big overall. They are a bit baggy through the back of the legs and butt in a less than flattering way, and I can easily pull the waistband away from my body at least an inch. I don't want to have to worry about them loosening throughout the day, and maybe putting them in the dryer will shrink them, but I'd rather just try on the next size down.

Nice quality, cute and comfortable! For reference, I'm 5'3" 145lbs and thick in the thighs. The size 8 fits perfectly! They seem to be good quality. I love the high waist and they are comfortable - bonus they are cute. Haven't noticed any stretch after wearing several hours. Love them!

Short, not capris length At 5’7”tall these are more like clam digger length on me rather than capris length. I’m also not in love with the fit because while the waist fits me perfectly (for reference 127 lbs 27” waist ordered size 4) there’s some rather odd bunching going on in the hip, leg and butt area that’s not shown in the model photos and the fit is somewhat a little tighter in the calf area. Material wise, I got the dark blue and although the material is pretty nice, not too thin or too thick, I’m not really liking the color too much, makes them look kind of cheap and again, doesn’t look like the same color as shown on the model. The description also lists these as mid rise but they are actually high rise and hit me just slightly below my belly button, not necessarily a bad thing if you prefer high rise but if you’re looking for mid rise, keep looking. What I liked, good stretch (no need to size up - if anything size down one, especially if your measurements fall in between the sizes noted in the sizing guide) , nice deep front pockets and they are comfortable and seem pretty durable. Bottom line: 3 stars mainly because the listing description and seller photos are somewhat misleading. These jeans are just okay. I’ll still wear from time to time but for casual outings and outdoor activities only.

Not flattering at all I am 5’2 and 160lbs - I ordered a size medium in bay blue. It fits my body ok, but is way to tight in my breast area (roughly a 36D). The buttons around the breasts are hard to fasten and you can see the gap where they are being pulled. The dress itself is also not very flattering and made me feel like I looked very frumpy. Perhaps it’s because of the babydoll style of it (the stretchy line runs right along my under boob). I think this dress would probably look best on thinner women with smaller breast, as the shape makes me look larger than I do in my other dresses. The color is nice and is a heavier jeans type material. I think I could probably make this dress work as a long open jacket, instead of buttoning it and wearing as a dress. As a dress though - this is definitely not for me.

Adorable, pretty dress! This is one of my favourite new dresses! I have been looking for a good denim dress, and I'm glad I picked this one! It's true-to-photos. Super Flattering and Comfortable Overalls If you're a toddler parent at this time then you know Halloween is all about dressing as Ms. Rachel. Since I could no longer fit into my pre-pregnancy overalls I love so much and got in Europe so can never replace, I went to Amazon for some "cheap" overalls to throw on for Halloween. I'm 5' 6" and 150lbs give or take a few lbs and I ordered the Medium and these fit perfectly! Not only do they fit perfectly but they're pretty darn flattering, for overalls, lol. The material is not thin, it looks and feels like I spent a good amount of money on them so I was pleasantly surprised. I had a few moms asking where I got them so from other perspectives they must be nice. I definitely feel like these are more durable than my favorite pair. They're not thick in the way that woukd limit motion, they're thick as in they don't feel like they'll tear when bending and moving around but they're not hot or stiff. I would definitely and have already recommended these to others.

Also for the girlies who feel like these might not look good with their body shape, im shaped like a freakin apple and they still looked cute👏🏼
