Quality is lacking, color is different I was excited when these overalls went on a flash sale as I had been going back and forth about whether to buy them ($45 for a pair of overalls is slightly expensive IMO, but I know pricing are continually rising for everything). I ordered the olive green color, and was disappointed when it came— it is definitely greenish, but seems more like a dark khaki on? Kind of giving safari ranger vibes, which was not what I was looking for. Second thing I noticed was that the quality was also lacking. While the fabric itself is very comfortable and soft, there are loose strings everywhere and a seam on the back was only partially finished. The length was good on me though— I adjusted the straps all the way and they came down right to my ankle, which doesn’t always happen as someone who is 5’4”. Unfortunately I’m returning because I very much dislike the color and the quality is not great.

They run a little short for longer legged ladies! I’m 5’7 (and a half 😉😆) and about 125-130lbs (depending on my snacking that day) and I ordered them in medium. I love the way they look and feel. Decent for the price I wanted something comfortable and cheerful for yard work. These are not perfect but good for the price. Don't be misled by the color. I would not describe it as brick red. The XL is sized just right for a working overall. L would have been too tight.

Best overalls ever! Fit great, very comfy. These overalls fit better, and are more comfortable, than any of the 6 other ones I own. Especially the fit across the butt is so good! For reference I wear a size 18 pants normally, and am 5’2”. These are an XXL. The legs are too long, but that’s true of every pair of pants I’ve ever bought, and is an easy fix. I just orders 2 more pairs in other colors.

Loved it, until I washed it When it arrived, the material was soft, like well worn denim. The fit was great, just roomy enough, not so tight that you can’t bend over. Quite a bit too long, but at 5’2”, I’m used to that. (The picture is before washing.) Then I washed them. Instructions said ‘cold water, do NOT tumble dry’, so that’s what I did. Yikes. I’d say they shrank somewhere between 1/2 to a full size. They ended up extremely wrinkled and the material became super stiff and rough, like the denim my Grandpa used to wear. I will try washing again and this time putting in the dryer; will update if they come out any better. Or worse.
Soft and Comfy I ordered the Cody Blue in Medium. I am 5'4" and 128#. They have just enough stretch and so comfortable and soft. Love them. I think I'll buy some more in other colors.

THE BEST OVERALLS I bought these for a family photoshoot and I’m obsessed! Love love love!!! They were stretchy enough to not feel constricted but still kept my figure! Not bulky and frumpy! 10/10 recommend! I am 155lbs 5’6” and got the size medium! ☺️

Very comfortable and roomy Very comfortable overalls! I am 5'0" and 125lbs and the small fits pretty well. The straps and pant legs are a little long but I expected that. Great fit, very comfortable I’ve never found overalls that fit as well as these. I’m 63 and am all about comfort. At 5’3” and 135 lb. I got a size medium and they’re a perfect fit. A little stretchy so definitely comfortable and let you move. I already ordered more in different colors.

Very cute, but run LARGE I’ll be adding photos of these on me soon. For now, I want to say I absolutely do love them, and want to wear them all day, every day. They have nice, deep pockets and are a nice weight Jean. True jeans, not some weird fabric. My only complaint/regret: But, I used the sizing chart, and knowing how wonky sizing can be on Amazon, I ordered 100% according to their size chart since I KNOW my measurements. They are HUGE. 😅 I’m swimming in them but they’re so comfortable that I still recommend them, just size down accordingly unless you’re really looking for that “painter/carpenter in overalls” look (nothing wrong with that!). I wish I had gotten a small or medium, and I got a large. My weight is 130 and I’m 5’2”. Also have to cuff the bottoms and have the buckles pretty high up and tight. I would love to be able to try the right size! The color truly reminded me 100% of painters overalls (brick), they are a burnt orange. If they had fit, the color would’ve been better. However, i decided to overdye them using RIT dye in Wine for about an hour. They took the dye beautifully and turned out a very deep red. I’m going to continue to dye them to keep them from getting too faded! UPDATED REVIEW: After having them for a little while, I wanted to add some notes. They are soo comfortable and I find myself wanting to wear them all the time. However, the pockets have Never laid quite right and this can really affect the way they look on. The front pocket bunches and leaves big gaps between my body and the overalls. The side pockets don’t want to fit correctly in their slots because due to the buttons pulling on them on each side, and the buttons aren’t even functional. I wash these by hand and hang them to dry, and even after steaming them, they still don’t look the way I want them to or as they are in the model photos. Maybe if the size chart had been more accurate I would’ve gotten the correct size and perhaps these issues would resolve themselves.

Thicker, flat blue color no fading I am 5'9, 108lbs, and usually would order a medium in overalls or jumpsuits to hope for extra length. Usually its not good enough and is still too short in the body and legs. I think I could've done with a small without sizing up because this is looser than expected from the product model photos. I have to roll mine because they are high water length on my ling legs. But because the legs are looser on my flamingo legs the rolling doesn't look the best on me. The front bib isn't too low with the straps adjusted to make the torso fit me. The color has zero fading add all in any areas, it's hard even to tell in my photos how very flat dyed blue they are. They are thicker and durable and better quality than even expected. There is enough room I think they could be maternity for a few months if you are built like me. I think they are a great value compared to name brands. These will work if you are taller like me, though not unrolled. I love that they are not so form fitting. They will be great in spring and autumn with a cardigan or sweatshirt. I recommend.

Cody blue bib overalls. Love them! I ordered these stretch denim bib overalls for outside work. I’m 5’5” and 135 lbs. I ordered a medium because I hoped they would fit well, but be a bit loose. There are two buckle straps, three side button closures, two side pockets, and a bib pocket. I had to roll up a small cuff on the medium. Machine wash on cold. They look very nice on and I think they will last a long time and maybe look better with age.

The jeans are nice but the sizing is tricky. I am wearing size XL with a 32" inseam. I usually wear size 12/14 and I would call these jeans size 16 based on how they fit. They fit comfortably in the waist and hips and they are just a little bit loose but I don't mind that. The problem is the length. I am wearing boots with 2" heels and I had to roll a double cuff to keep the the legs from dragging on the ground. After I write this review, I'm going to hem up these pants. Fit exactly as I expected, great quality for price I’m 5’7 165-170 pounds, and I ordered a large. it’s not super stretchy but it’s thick material and feels high quality, color is light blue as I got the Sunday light blue, i will say the blue is slightly darker than the picture/ in person but its a very slight difference, may just be my eyes. I have the sleeves rolled here and they are still perfect length. It is slightly cropped which was the design I selected. I buttoned it up and it was snug but still had room to move, I plan on keeping it unbuttoned. It Had kind of a weird smell when opening it but nothing a good wash won’t fix. I recommend following the washing instructions unless you want it to shrink. I love it honestly.

Sooo stretchy I’m 5 feet tall and 115 lbs and the extra small was perfect. I have to cuff my sleeves (because I’m a shortie) but that gives the jacket a little extra style. I love how stretchy this jacket is. Be forewarned that if you buy it be prepared to invest time in getting the smell out of it. As others have noted, it arrives smelling like it’s been in someone’s attic or musty basement for 50 years. I washed it three times. Before the last time I washed it I sprayed Odoban on it (generously) and let it sit in the sun before the last time I washed it. That helped considerably but the smell is there although it’s very faint. Think of it as the price you pay for a good deal and you’ll be less salty about the smell.

Soft Comfy Denim, Darker Color than Expected The denim fabric of the - FTCayanz Women's Baggy Jeans Pull On Denim Joggers Elastic Waist Casual Harem Pants with Pockets – is very soft making these pants very comfortable to wear. The fabric is on the thin side so the pants fall and drape nicely. The color of the denim fabric, in person seems to be darker with less fading than it appears in the listing’s photographs. Not a deal breaker, it’s just that I would have liked more fading. The fit is great. The length is long for my 5’4” frame but I like the cuffed up look. The waistline pockets are roomy but the only back pocket, is small. I would have preferred two larger back pockets. Interesting looking aglets (see attached photo). Overall, these are cute and comfy baggy joggers and I like them a lot. For sizing reference: My Waist Measures 38", My Hips Measure 48”. I ordered the size 2XL

LOVE these jeans! I absolutely love these jogger style jeans. These are great dupes of similar style jeans I’ve purchased from Free People. I’m 5’5,” 165lbs, 35” waist, 44” hips and my thighs are 23” around. I would normally order XL, but per the size guide provided I decided to go with Large and I think the fit is fantastic. I typically like my jeans oversized and these fit loosely but not crazy slouchy or sloppy looking. The elasticized waist is not binding or too tight (a huge dealbreaker for me). The drawstring actually works; it’s not just decorative.The quality is excellent and I especially love the fabric - it’s a great weight. Something I can wear year round here in South Florida. I washed these in cold and dried on low. I was worried these would shrink but I couldn’t detect any. These are so on trend and a lot more budget friendly than my FP ones. I’m thinking of purchasing a darker color in XL solely for the length. While I like the inseam of these, I like having the option of double or triple cuffing my legs. I recommend using the size guide as your baseline fit. These are meant to worn baggy, but everyone’s preference is different..

WEDGIE "SHUT UP" WASH SIZE COMPARISON Because I do not usually spend this kind of money on clothing and am pretty unreliable with completing online returns, I read every review I could find on multiple websites before I selected my size. I still got it wrong (lol). I don’t usually leave reviews, but there is a LOT of conflicting information about the sizing of these jeans out there and I want to share what I found to be true.When selecting your size, I recommend taking two factors into consideration:1. What is your body shape/type – i.e. where do you carry your weight? If you are someone with more of an hourglass shape or more thick in the thighs/butt, I would follow the guidance of most of the reviews and size up. If you are someone like me with a small/medium butt and legs, I would recommend selecting either your normal size, or one size down.2. How do you want the jeans to fit – i.e. do you want a relaxed fit or a tighter fit? For reference, I am around 5’7, 122 pounds, and usually a size 26/size 2 in pants. The size 26 Wedgies fit me perfectly right out of the bag. However, based on other reviewers’ experience, 100% cotton jeans will relax/mold to your body over time and use. If I kept the size 26, they would have evolved to more of a “boyfriend” style relaxed fit on me. I personally wanted a snugger fit. I then ordered the size 25. They were tight on me and not the most comfortable right out of the bag (they also made my butt look smaller which was the opposite of what I was hoping). However, after wearing them for a few hours, especially sitting in them, they relaxed and fit me exactly the way I envisioned.I attached pictures of me in both and indicated the size I am wearing in each photo. This is what each pair looked like immediately after opening the package - I did not take a photo of the size 25 after they relaxed, but trust me when I say, they're worth it! Hope this helps someone!

WEDGIE "SHUT UP" WASH SIZE COMPARISON Because I do not usually spend this kind of money on clothing and am pretty unreliable with completing online returns, I read every review I could find on multiple websites before I selected my size. I still got it wrong (lol). I don’t usually leave reviews, but there is a LOT of conflicting information about the sizing of these jeans out there and I want to share what I found to be true.When selecting your size, I recommend taking two factors into consideration:1. What is your body shape/type – i.e. where do you carry your weight? If you are someone with more of an hourglass shape or more thick in the thighs/butt, I would follow the guidance of most of the reviews and size up. If you are someone like me with a small/medium butt and legs, I would recommend selecting either your normal size, or one size down.2. How do you want the jeans to fit – i.e. do you want a relaxed fit or a tighter fit? For reference, I am around 5’7, 122 pounds, and usually a size 26/size 2 in pants. The size 26 Wedgies fit me perfectly right out of the bag. However, based on other reviewers’ experience, 100% cotton jeans will relax/mold to your body over time and use. If I kept the size 26, they would have evolved to more of a “boyfriend” style relaxed fit on me. I personally wanted a snugger fit. I then ordered the size 25. They were tight on me and not the most comfortable right out of the bag (they also made my butt look smaller which was the opposite of what I was hoping). However, after wearing them for a few hours, especially sitting in them, they relaxed and fit me exactly the way I envisioned.I attached pictures of me in both and indicated the size I am wearing in each photo. This is what each pair looked like immediately after opening the package - I did not take a photo of the size 25 after they relaxed, but trust me when I say, they're worth it! Hope this helps someone!

HOLY BUTT-GRAIL JEANS I’ve had a vision of what I want my jeans to do and look like on me and I finally found them! They’re PERFECT! So perfect that I can’t stop sneaking a peek at my butt any time I walk past a mirror in these lol. I am 5’5”, about 125 lbs, waist measures 27”, hips/butt measure 39”, and these are a size 26 in Tango Light. Before Levi’s, my go to jeans were American Eagle size 2 for a snug fit or 4 for a comfortable relaxed fit. According to the Levi’s size chart, my measurements put me in size 26 for my waist and size 27 for my hips. I have a pair of originals in size 27 because they are 100% cotton and I was afraid buying to fit my waist would flatten my butt, but they have relaxed really nicely to more of a boyfriend look. Seeing how well the 100% cotton originals stretched, I felt confident buying the Wedgies in size 26 and I’m glad I did. They *feel* like 100% cotton, they hold in and support like 100% cotton, but that 1% of elastane makes bending over and bending at the knee so much more comfortable, and they fit beautifully. A tiny bit of butt flattening at first, but once I wore them around the house for an hour they were perfect!

Cute, but uncomfortable. I'm really torn on these jeans. While they look cute on my butt, I found them to be pretty uncomfortable and don't love the price. The button fly paired with the stiffer denim, plus the fact that these were probably a size too small on me, made them kind of miserable to wear. For reference, I'm currently anywhere from an 8 to a 12 in jeans, depending on the brand/fit/material, and my measurements are currently 44"-32"-44". I'm 5'3" with a larger butt and thighs, with a short torso and longer legs. I usually buy regular length pants because short tends to be just a tad too short for my taste, but still too long to be that perfect ankle length... Hopefully that makes sense. I got these in a SIZE 30 in the color TANGO LIGHT. As you can see in the photos, the color is lovely but they're a bit tight on me. I suspected I may need a 31 but hoped they would relax a little after a few hours of wear and become the perfect snug fit in the 30. While that may be the case, I honestly don't know if I like them enough to be miserable for the hours it'll take to make that happen. I will say that the Premium material is a bit softer than the other stiffer Levi's jeans, like the standard Wedgie Skinnies. They still aren't soft though, so if that's what you're looking for, I would say these aren't for you. I'd recommend these for someone looking for a sturdier denim with little to no give. I'm sure they will last a long time. I ended up returning these and am unsure if I'll ever bother to try a 31. I've decided I'm not really a lover of the button fly. Oh well.

Unisex style is great quality I really love this burgundy wine belt. I ordered it in the 38-40" size to fit my 40" waist and it fits both at the natural waist and with jeans (womens plus size 18w), so I would say it runs very true to size. The leather is top-grain, genuine leather, which is often difficult to find, and the style is pretty unisex. This isn't a super wide belt, which means it will fit into the belt loops of women's pants, making it very wearable by both men and women. The hardware is heavy-duty, and the antique brass color is very attractive. The belt comes with the tools necessary to adjust the length, which is a nice bonus. My daughters admired it as soon as they saw it, and we all love the tasteful but somewhat out-of-the-ordinary wine color. Good quality + great appearance = a winner!

It looks like it would be really easy to shorten it, which I may do eventually. It did come with a punch tool to add or enlarge holes and make a new hole if you shorten it. I can see enlarging the center of the flower design to make more size options. Use your pants waist size to order, I wear small (5' 4" 130lbs), size 2 or 4 women's pants depending on brand and cut. I ordered B-Black size 26-35. I'm left with about 6-7 inches of belt past the buckle. The buckle was covered in plastic wrap to protect it in shipping, nice touch. I am likely going to order this in other design and colors. Really happy with this!!

I've gotten other brands of stretchy jeans before that did. If you're just in one of the moods where you want to be super comfortable that day.. like gardening, or casual lunch or grocery shopping... I'd definitely Recommend these stretchy jeans...

Flattering!! (sorry for the blurry mirror) For reference I’m 5’4” 135lbs and a 31 year old mom. I ordered these first in size 4/27 (my normal Levi’s size) but they were too big. I re-ordered in size 2/26 and they fit perfectly. I am normally not a size 2/26 so they ran large on me. They are extremely flattering on my pear shape. NO gap in the back of the waist! I have a thick bottom half (and short legs) and I don’t like to wear short shorts. These fit really nice! Nice mid to high rise on them. You can see in my picture it comes to about my belly button. You don’t have to worry about bending over and showing plumbers butt. The dark wash is really nice and classy looking. The pocket placement is great. They are overall extremely flattering. A little bit of stretch, but not too much. Pictures taken when the shorts were new but they’ve been washed and worn since with no issues to the actual shorts. My only issue was with the denim wash. I did have to use a color catcher while washing. It still dyed one of my white hand towels light blue! Be careful of color transfer with the dark wash! The only thing I wish was the bottom leg holes of the shorts was a tiny bit smaller so it hugged my knees a little tighter. I’m just picky! The bottom is not sewn into place so you have to roll the shorts when you get them out of the dryer. They are really flattering on the backside and I definitely recommend them for pear shapes like me. I love not having to pull shorts down from riding up when I walk. That is why I like bike shorts and Bermuda shorts. If you have big thighs you know what I mean. I think curvy girls in particular will love these. The quality on mine is very good! I am definitely going to stock up and order a bunch for summer. I wish they had more colors in my size. Hopefully this review was to someone out there!

Got size 27.

Nice summer skirt Model is 5’6”, 160 lbs. wearing size Large.This is a nice, practical summer cargo skirt. The fit is true to size - maybe slightly small in the waist, but only slightly. The material feels well made. It is a nice casual and practical but still feminine look for the summer months.

Raw hem makes it easy to shorten to size I'm 5'5" and these jeans were still a little long for me. I was able to undo the side hem and cut about 1 inch off the bottom. They were also not that tapered at the end so they looked more like bootcut than skinny jeans. I was able to take it in along with closing up the edge hem. They fit right now but definitely took some tailoring skills. The high waist fits fine. They have some stretch to them so they're comfortable enough.

Too long for petites. SIZE DOWN (stretches out) I really liked these jeans until I started wearing them all day. They stretch out SO MUCH, and the waistband is much lower than I expected for a high-rise jean. I’m short so I knew the split hem would be too long, but rolled up they look great. Since it’s a raw hem, I might just cut them off. I’m 5’0”, 148lbs, and normally wear an 8 short in GAP or Abercrombie jeans. I ordered an 8 and they’re actually too big! They’re best for curvy figures; roomy in the waist, hips, crotch and butt. Plenty of stretch and wiggle room. I’m afraid if I sized down, they’d be too snug in the thighs. The fabric is really soft and feels similar to Abercrombie or American Eagle denim, but unfortunately the waistband feels a little scratchy and uncomfortable. Another issue is the back pocket placement. Instead of lifting my bigger booty up, they tend to pull it down and flatten it out. The back pockets are super low! Size down if you’re between sizes, or size up for a looser fit. Hope this helps! 🙂

Pocket was unraveling after first wash. I ordered a medium. I am 5' 4" so the length was perfect with some platform sneakers. They are bulky around the waist. They looked fine when I first put them on but sadly the butt became baggy in a very short time.

Very pleasantly surprised how good these fit I am 5’3” and about 150# athletic build usually a size 27 or 28 in jeans and got the medium in these and they fit perfectly - actually I am very pleasantly surprised how well they fit and how comfortable they are. I have a 25” inseam and these are also the perfect length for me. The barrel cut doesn’t look very barrely on me but I’m not sure if that is because of my build - I have large thighs and butt. I am happy with the cut though as I have tried some barrel jeans that are very circus-ish. These are a good in between. The denim quality is ok, I have some expensive jeans and the denim quality isn’t up there with those but for the price I didn’t expect it to be. The distressing is fun and works well. The shoelace belt thing is a bit ordinary so I may look to switch that out to elevate them a bit. Overall I am extremely happy with these for the price and feel like I will wear them a lot.

12% polyester These run pretty true to size - maybe ever-so-slightly small. I ordered my usual size medium, and they fit tightly but comfortable. I did not put them in the dryer, just in case, I didn't want any shrinkage. The label also says to hang dry, but they are cotton 88% and polyester 12%, so I don't see why you couldn't dry these. Speaking of the material, I am disappointed that they have any polyester since the listing claims 100% cotton. My skin has an aversion to polyester, so I try to keep it out of my wardrobe. These feel decently soft on my skin, so I am hoping they will be ok, but I am taking off 1 star due to the claim of 100% cotton, since that is not what the label says. Other than the material, these are just like the like the listing photos. They are baggy but a flattering cut. I am 5'3" and these come below my ankles, but they are not too long. I'm excited to put together some artsy outfits with these!

Magical Mystery Size I could not find ANY sizing information for these jeans anywhere. I even went to the LifeShe store. I guessed an XL and I think they fit pretty much the way I wanted them to so I guess I was lucky. I'm 5'7" and about 170 with 43" hips. They are loose and comfy. Also be aware that, while there is NO spandex (so NO give) these are also NOT 100% cotton (as they are described). The inner label clearly states 12% polyester and, while I really don't care about that, I wish the description had been accurate. All in all they are nice jeans for everyday

Short but VERY comfortable! These are possibly the most comfortable pants I own. They're very lightweight and stretchy. In my opinion it has a minor issue, but nothing I can’t overlook. ***** A GREAT product/item. I love it…I’d definitely reorder it and I highly recommend it! I have absolutely nothing negative to say about it. I saw these Sweezarmo denim stretch joggers with an elasticized drawstring waistband and ordered them in my usual size small after consulting the company’s provided size chart. The Sky Blue color is represented accurately in the description photos, and the size runs true to the chart measurements. In person, the fabric looks like a true pair of denim jeans and to touch, feels like regular denim….with a little stretch. I was surprised at how much stretch they have in them. The weight of the denim is even close to what I’d expect for a pair of summer jeans. Prior to receiving them, I had some was concern about the waist having enough stretch, since my waist measurement is at least a couple inches larger than the posted measurement on the size chart. Sometimes elasticized waistbands can be constricting without much stretch, but that’s not the case with these. For those with a smaller waist, the drawstring is functional and can be cinched up for the right fit. On me, I feel the pants make my hips look big…but maybe some of that’s due to me being fuller in the waist!?! One feature I absolutely love about the pants is the pockets. Two in the front and two on the back. The front pockets are nice and deep for slipping hands or a cell phone in. I feel the pants are comfortable to wear and on the right figure (i.e. models in the listing photos) they look adorable! The quality of these is good from the materials used to the craftsmanship in the stitching. The pants are actually long enough that I’d be comfortable wearing them with sandals or flats without cuffing them, or rolling them up once or twice for an ankle or cropped look. Due to the thickness of the denim fabric, the elasticized waistband is a little bulky looking and feeling, so I think cropped tops or tops tucked in look the best with these bottoms. Overall I’m pleased with the denim pants (I personally don’t refer to them as joggers). They’re comfortable and wash up well, following care instructions. I will get wear out of them, but they won’t be bottoms I wear out and about often. They are so cute, but just not flattering on my body build. I appreciate the opportunity to try these, but in seeing how they look on me, I probably wouldn’t order them again for the price. But, for others that are considering them, I would recommend them for their quality and suggest staying true to size. For reference, my measurements are: Height - 5’4” Bust - 35” Waist - 30” Arm length - 22.5” Shoulder width - 15” Weight - 120# Inseam - 31”

Prior to receiving them, I had some was concern about the waist having enough stretch, since my waist measurement is at least a couple inches larger than the posted measurement on the size chart. Sometimes elasticized waistbands can be constricting without much stretch, but that’s not the case with these. For those with a smaller waist, the drawstring is functional and can be cinched up for the right fit. On me, I feel the pants make my hips look big…but maybe some of that’s due to me being fuller in the waist!?! One feature I absolutely love about the pants is the pockets. Two in the front and two on the back. The front pockets are nice and deep for slipping hands or a cell phone in. I feel the pants are comfortable to wear and on the right figure (i.e. models in the listing photos) they look adorable! The quality of these is good from the materials used to the craftsmanship in the stitching. The pants are actually long enough that I’d be comfortable wearing them with sandals or flats without cuffing them, or rolling them up once or twice for an ankle or cropped look. Due to the thickness of the denim fabric, the elasticized waistband is a little bulky looking and feeling, so I think cropped tops or tops tucked in look the best with these bottoms. Overall I’m pleased with the denim pants (I personally don’t refer to them as joggers). They’re comfortable and wash up well, following care instructions. I will get wear out of them, but they won’t be bottoms I wear out and about often. They are so cute, but just not flattering on my body build. I appreciate the opportunity to try these, but in seeing how they look on me, I probably wouldn’t order them again for the price. But, for others that are considering them, I would recommend them for their quality and suggest staying true to size. For reference, my measurements are: Height - 5’4” Bust - 35” Waist - 30” Arm length - 22.5” Shoulder width - 15” Weight - 120# Inseam - 31”

COMFORTABLE, FLATTERING JEANS AT A GREAT PRICE I am 5'4" and weigh 173 lbs and purchased the medium and they fit great; just the way that I like them. The ad says they are bell bottom, which they are clearly not; and for that, I am grateful for. I almost didn't buy them based on that description; I am not a fan of bell bottoms. These jeans have a great amount of stretch and look so flattering on a curvier figure, however they're so forgiving, they would look great on any figure. I would LOVE it if this style pant came in white or other color options; I'd buy them in a heartbeat. The price for these jeans at the time of this review is $29.99 with 20% off. That's a GREAT price. I paid $50 for a different brand that fit similar to these; but I actually prefer this brand. I totally recommend these jeans.

I'm 67, 5'7" and 118 pounds. Size 2 fit ok with a belt. At first, I thought they'd be fine, and was going to get another pair. We have a farm animal sanctuary and good work pants are essential. Then I washed them twice (according to instructions) and they are so badly pilled that they look like I got them from the bargain bin at a thrift store. Color not as faded, kinda tight in the crotch I sized up in these shorts since I hate it when they feel too tight or too short. Large fits comfortably without digging in, and I get extra length. Unfortunately, it kind of gives me 🐫 toe… For ref, I’m 5’0”, 150 lbs, 36D & got the brown color in Large. luckily they’re SO stretchy! They’re less of a denim material and more of a stretchy knit fabric. They’re very thin and stretch out as you wear them, but washing and drying them shrinks them back to their original size and shape. The craftsmanship and quality aren’t the best; I’m seeing loose threads all over and some seams are unraveling after a few washes. Ugh. It also isn’t the same brown I saw online. In person it’s more of a UPS uniform color, 😂 or a poop emoji copper-brown shade…. I’m hoping it doesn’t wash me out when I have a tan this summer. I won’t reorder and I probably won’t wear them much.

Short but mighty Sizing info first off: I'm 5'1" and weigh 125. I usually wear a 2 or a 4, or a Small, in bottoms. I ordered these overalls in a S and that seems to have been about right. I could probably fit into an XS to get it looking less baggy at the waist, but wouldn't get the overall slumpy look I was going for then. With that out of the way, let's go over some cons: my main complaint is that these are much shorter than I anticipated them being. That's not to say they're obscenely short or anything, but when I sit down I feel like these are Very Short. I'd guess these have maybe a 4" inseam? A second con is that the straps are...flimsy isn't quite the right word, as the fabric appears to be of a good thickness, but the length sliders don't move easily, and when they do, the fabric of the straps folds and clumps up, making adjusting the straps even harder. Now, some pros: the fabric seems to be real denim, not a polyester imitation, and has a good thickness to it. The stitching on the folded leg hems seems strong and more than adequate. These run true to size and the color I received matches the color in the listing. The hip pockets are real pockets, big enough to fit my phone, which in this day and age of fake pockets, is a big deal to me. Overall, if you don't mind a short inseam and somewhat annoying straps, these are a solid buy. I anticipate them lasting well.

Cute and comfy! UPDATE 2: third year in a row buying these shorts for summer they are my favorite! I wanted to give an update on sizing because I’ve been working out and lost weight. I am now 5’4” 121lbs and size 2 fits perfectly! Although my shorts from the last 2 years are still in great shape they don’t fit anymore. These shorts last for a long time. :) I am buying 8 new pairs for this summer in size 2 because they are literally all I wear lol! I used to hate shorts but with these they cover my cellulite and give me confidence! No more long pants in the summer. Yay! I also started to order my step daughter these because her school dress code requires girls to wear longer shorts and she likes them as well. She’s 5’3” 135-140 with a straight figure and she likes the way size 4 fits. UPDATE 1: I ordered these last summer I think? I still am loving these shorts! I am back to order more for upcoming warm weather here in Texas. Black is finally back in stock- yay! I ordered a black pair and love them. They fit the same as the dark wash jean ones I have. I actually think I like the black ones even more than the dark denim, I didn’t think that was possible! Needless to say I definitely recommend these. I’m ordering now because when warm weather comes these sell out like hot cakes! Get yours now ladies! They have become mostly the only shorts I wear besides Nike athletic shorts for working out. I need to order multiples because I wear them constantly. Not having to pull your shorts down from riding up between your legs all day is amazing. Curvy ladies will know what I mean! I’ve tried so many other shorts and none of them compare to these. The only Bermuda shorts I’ve ever found that are flattering on me and don’t make my short legs look ridiculously frumpy! I wish they had even more washes for me to order because white and light wash aren’t my thing, not flattering on my big thighs. I only wear medium, dark or black wash denim. Although I’m tempted to go outside my comfort zone and possibly try the light wash.... ****Original review: I’m 5’4” 135ish and I ordered size 6. I range between a size 4-6 or 27-28. I’m normally a 4 in jeans. Size 6 is comfortable, ever so slightly loose. The size 4 fits as well (I tried both) but I just preferred the size 6 because it had a little more wiggle room. The pictures of me posted in the shorts are of the size 6. A lot of the reviews said to go up a size and I can see why because the size 4 was pretty fitted on me. I don’t like my shorts very tight fitting. Unless you are not curvy or very slim, then you may not need to go with your larger size. Or maybe you want a super tight fitting look, in that case you could probably stick with your normal size. They are extremely comfortable and flattering especially in the dark wash. I am a 30 year old toddler mom and these are very cute and practical for me! I don’t like short shorts so these are a win win. I don’t have to worry about them riding up and pulling them down during the day. Ladies who have thick thighs that rub, a big bottom and/or mommy tummy these are for you! I’m so glad to have found some comfy and cute longer length shorts that look flattering. They have a nice stretch and they don’t give me muffin top or cut into my stomach. I think curvy and/or plus size girls will love these too! I ordered like 5 pairs because I love them so much lol! The only slight issue I had was they stretched out a little bit when I wore them, but it wasn’t a big issue. They weren’t totally bagging out or anything. The only other thing would be I wish the back pockets were blank with no wavy line, not a big deal, just personal preference. Overall they just fit my daily casual mom style. I like how the hem is finished with a little roll, makes them look more pulled together in my opinion. They look cute with tanks, tees or even long sleeves! I like how they have a skinny leg, otherwise Bermuda shorts tend to look frumpy on me if they aren’t fitted to the leg. Levi’s always comes through for me. These definitely look cute and classy and more expensive then the price tag! If you hate short shorts you need these! They are a total confidence booster for those of us who want to hide cellulite, big thighs or just prefer a little more coverage. No inner thighs sticking together in the heat with the longer length lol! Lastly, you can’t beat the price!!

Comfortable, flattering, cut the hems to make longer I have come back twice for these shorts already this year, and so long as Levis doesn't change them, I will keep coming back! These shorts are incredibly comfortable and quite flattering. They have just enough stretch and don't loosen with wear. I am 5'8"/5'9"ish and sit between a 12 and 14 so I ordered 14 to keep it comfy and they are perfect! I could probably get away with a 12, but I didn't want to chance a muffin top look. With my rear end, I usually end up with a gap at the back of a lot of jeans/shorts, but these stay flat across my lower back.I DO cut the stitches with a seam ripper and unroll the hem to make them a bit longer. The serged hemline looks great and doesn't show any signs of coming loose. It takes a little work so you don't pick at the wrong seams, but the trouble is totally worth it!All in all, these are excellent shorts, they always seems to be well priced (when in stock!), and I highly recommend them for girls with curves!

New favorite belt - simple, and classy! My new favorite belt. A simple belt really upgrades regular outfits for the office and ties them together! This one is sleek, clean, and cut well. I’ve always either inherited belts from family or wore utilitarian ones that came with jeans and needed to replace my wide black one which no longer fit…I think this is the nicest fashion belt I’ve owned so I’m very delighted at the quality for this price - particularly how smoothly it slides into place and holds. I wear this belt (30-35”) at my waist / stomach and I like the slightly wider appearance to balance my broad shoulders and bust in relation to my waist. It did not slip down or loosen throughout the day. Buy the size you are currently with no hesitation - it comes with 7 holes and a simple piercing tool to add additional notches if you ever lose weight, which was a pleasant surprise! My current measurements are 165-170lbs, 34.5” waist, 38.5” belly/midsection and it is very comfortable. I will update if the PU leather starts flaking but it seems well made. Cute Style, Flattering Cut, Comfortable Stretch, Sturdy Denim I ordered these jeans in color Blue, size 8. They fit me just the same as most name brand jeans of that size fit me. The waist is high yet well-proportioned in that it doesn't rise above my belly button like some other high-waisted pants I've purchased. I like where the hemline hits my legs. For reference, I'm 5'4". The fabric is substantial in weight and has the feel of a very comfortable, stretchy cotton denim. I've put these jeans through 5 laundry cycles using cold water and one brief drying cycle on medium heat and although they did shrink a bit, it didn't affect the fit much because the fabric is stretchy. I think the cut of these jeans is both stylish and flattering, and the wide pant legs allow lots of freedom of motion in the thigh area. There was one hiccup, however. The jeans arrived carrying a strong odor in the fabric, which to me smells like petroleum. This is the reason I took off one star. I've now put them through 5 wash cycles, one brief dryer cycle and hung them outside in the sunshine. I'd researched that baking soda, vinegar and/or lemon juice might help, so I used those in the last wash cycle and it did help to a degree.

Comparison: Joe’s and Paige I love the material. It has just enough stretch. It doesn’t feel like it will slip off or like it’s too structured. I was nervous ordering these because my experience with joes has always been that they run small in my thighs. I ordered a size up and the fit on my thighs and waist was perfect. I have two issues with these pants 1) way too long and 2) makes my butt look flat. I usually only wear Paige Jeans size 26, but I must say that I like the material on these jeans more than Paige. The Paige jeans are even softer than these and hug my body without feeling at all uncomfortable or tight; however, they don’t maintain their shape very well after a bunch of washes. The bottoms start to roll up and the fabric doesn’t look as good (almost like how wool starts to get fuzzy). Also, I like fit at the waist better on these jeans than any of my Paige jeans. I like that these give me a little more support in the stomach area without feeling tight and I don’t need to worry about bending down and exposing myself. I might just be getting older and prefer a sexy but covered up look over the Paige jeans which have a lower rise and are less forgiving. I’m giving these three stars because I love the material, but they just don’t look on my petite body frame. For reference, I’m 5’2 and 125 pounds.

Nice thick denim These are super comfortable pants. I did get the extra large and there are a little bit on the big side. I'm not a pregnant individual. I am 5'7, 190 lb. So, I'm a chunky lady. So, I thought a pair of pants like this would give me that little extra around the top. I wish I would have ordered the L instead of the XL. It's a little big around the legs and a little bit loose on the waist. But, the length is good and the quality is there. They're a nice boot cut so you can wear them with pretty much any kind of shoe . These are nice jeans. I'm still going to wear them even though they're a little bit loose and I'll just add a belt. It'll look great with a sweater. I like the fact that they are thicker and we're going into a season that's cold. They might be a little heavy for summer time. But all in all, they are really nice jeans. If you have a bigger tummy or you're pregnant it'll be nice because you've got that little extra elastic on the sides. I pulled it out the top to see how far it would go and it really had a lot of give.

Shorter Inseam, Overall Skinny Fit These seem well suitable for 1st and 2nd trimester for most people, I think. They are a little firmer elastic than I expected so I think it will depend on the person since some people gain more inches on their waist during a pregnancy than others. As far as I can tell, I think these will comfortably come out as far as 13 inches past your pre-pregnancy waist, and maybe a few inches more, depending on your tolerance towards tighter elastic feeling. However I think these are really great for apple-shaped people or who suffer from extreme bloating. They hit right on the natural waist and with a longer top that covers the elastic, they look just like fashionable jeans. Actually these are probably the most flattering jeans I've ever worn, they make my legs look soooo skinny. I usually buy jeans in size 10 - and these in size Small are like a size 6 on the legs part and like from size 8 out to like a good 22 or so for the waist. The unstretched waist is 30 inches, the rise is 9.5", the inseam is actually 27" (and I am 5'3" so this is perfect for me). Unstretched hip is 36", they stretch out fine to my 38" hips. My waist size fluctuates a lot and then it's not unusual for me to bloat out as much as 10 inches by dinner so these are so great for me. I'll see how they hold up to more washings but I'm probably going to try out more from this brand. The only cons I really see so far is the lack of front pockets and that the inside thread feels a bit scratchy in places.

So many mixed reviews. Here’s mine… I wasn’t sure what size to get. A lot of people saying it was too big others saying it was too small, I just went with my gut. I’m usually a Medium but I’m so tricky because I’m 5’1 and curvy. Current weight is 150lbs, bra size 36D, jeans between 28-29. I ordered a Large hoping it would be a nice lose fit like the model. The Large is perfect! The dress has a pretty significant gap in between buttons in my opinion so this size helps with the gaps not opening up and revealing my body parts or inside garments lol. It is thin and a bit see through but not horrible. The length was just what I wanted! I can’t wait to wear it. It was a bit smelly but I wash all my clothes before wearing them so that’s the least of my worries. A bit pricey for the quality yes, but the fact that I didn’t have to go shopping in the search of a denim like dress pays off. I know a real denim dress would cost more and weigh more. This is light weight and works for any season! Also, the fit exactly what I was hoping for, it arrived the next day and saved tomorrow’s event. If you wanted to be fitted it will be tricky to find the right size for you unless you have a stick figure body. This is meant to fit like the model in my opinion! I’m happy with my purchase. Pattern makes up for deficits These are cute and I'll definitely wear them often. They're a tiny bit big but I anticipated that since there was no size 0 (5'6" ~115 25/26 inch waist). The waist fits okay, but my waist is thicker for my frame. The thighs are a little baggy, but that's my life. I have very narrow legs. I feel like if they were not patterned the slightly off fit might look sloppier, but since they are I'm fine with it. I have trouble with jeans being too tight in the waist if they fit elsewhere, so these are actually more comfortable for me. For reference, I have bought the smallest size jeans available to accommodate my little booty and legs but feel absolutely unwell from how tight they are in the waist (specifically mostly cotton madewell jeans in a 23). The button is a little cheap/loose as others have mentioned on this brands other listings. I don't think it will be an issue for me, but if they fit my waist more snuggly it would be. I'm 5'6" and they're perfect length for tennis shoes, but if you're any shorter you'll need heels. They have a frayed hem, but there is a hem stitch above it. So you could cut it, but I don't think it would look the same. These are definitely stretchy jeans. Wish they had a slightly higher cotton content because I fear baggy knees in my future, but like I said, the pattern helps them look sleeker than they are. I got lots of compliments. Decent value and free people dupe.

Cute But Sizing Chart is Off, Read Description for Correct Size These are some unique and cute jeans that can be dressed up or down. And they are long leg friendly!! Yes! It's hard finding pants long enough for my 32.5" inseam. I can actually wear low heels with these jeans. I ordered a Size 6 according to the sizing chart in the photos but these pants are too big. I usually wear a size 2 or 4. I am 5'10, 123 lbs, 32B-26-35 for reference. So with those measurements a size 6 should fit but these jeans are too big in the waist, butt and hip area. If you read the product description, the sizing they list there is probably more correct as they list a Size 2 with a 26" waist and 34" hips. Wish I would have read this instead of using the sizing chart in the photos. Also these are very high waisted. I have a really high waist, basically have no torso, all legs and in order for me not to have a droopy looking crotch area, I had to pull these up really high. Luckily, I know how to alter clothing to fit me the way I like so I will wash them first to see if there's any shrinkage and then take the pants in as needed.

Check, Please! These green and white checkered pants are adorable, comfortable and unique. Even though they're available on Amazon, you're unlikely to end up in a room with anyone else wearing them. The denim is on the thinner side if you compare them with typical jeans (Levis, etc.) but they're not too lightweight to wear in average NYC fall temperatures. They're a slightly stretchy, medium-wight twill. I ordered a 6 based on the size chart but should have listened to Amazon's advice when they said to get a 4. If Amazon makes a recommendation, I would follow it over the manufacturer's size chart in this instance. For reference, I'm 5'8", 110 lbs, 26" waist, 35" hip, 33" inseam. I do like the length, although according to the size chart it's the same for the 4 and 6. It's a 32" inseam according to my measurement but feels longer. I'm not accounting for the rise, which is average. I expected it to be shorter as I assumed these were going to be cut like junior's pants, but to my relief they're not. Maybe I'll wash them in warm water and tumble dry to tighten them up a bit. They're 98% cotton so they should shrink somewhat if you want them to. However, the green dye is already running into the white so it might be best to use cold water. I love the flare—they're as close to bell bottoms as I've seen in this century. They're wider than a boot cut unless the footwear in question is moon boots. They're unhemmed and have fashionably frayed ends but there's a line of subtle stitches 1/4" above the bottom to keep the fabric from further fraying. There are five functional pockets in all: two traditional jean pockets on the rump and two stitched round pockets in front, plus one small coin pocket, and there are belt loops that can accommodate a range of belt widths. For such an unusual, comfortable pair of jeans $39.99 seems reasonable if you like the way they fit. Again, my only concern is that the green dye will bleed into the white.

True to size These pants are very high waisted, have decent pockets for female pants with a frayed, wide flare leg. The denim has a touch of elastic to it so they hug well without needing to do gymnastics to get them on. I was impressed that not only were they long enough but at 5’10”, touched the floor barefooted, something most pants never do. The zipper is metal and well sewn in, the rivets are all well pressed but the button is just a snap on which means it’s loose on the fabric and will not last overly long. They wash well without shrinking, keeping their vivid white and black pattern but the frayed bottom did fray a lot more. May need either a stitch line or some fray stop to keep them from falling apart long term.

Fabulous and Fun I fell in love with these funky checkerboard jeans the minute I tried them on. I’m 5’9, 160 lb, 29–30” waist, and I normally wear a size 10. Looking at the size chart, I decided to order size 12, and it was a perfect fit. I need a 32” inseam minimum for flat shoes, and bless these pants, because they have a 32.5” inseam. They have a ragged hem but are stitched so they are durable (see picture). If you want a shorter hem, it would be easy to cut them; if you do, I’d either stitch the edges or use iron-on hemming tape. They have a little stretch and are comfortable and hold their shape all day. I got the black and white, so I wasn’t worried about washing them in the machine. They have laundered well on a cold, gentle cycle and hang to dry.

Not the product in the picture For reference, I am 5’6” 142 lbs with a rectangular torso and thick thighs, I ordered a medium, as suggested, and I don’t have any complaints about the way they fit. They are snug on my body type, because I don’t have much of a waist, but if you are on the skinny side or have an hourglass figure, i’m sure it would be different. There’s nothing overtly wrong with the shorts, but they simply aren’t what I ordered. I included a picture of the color and style for reference, next to what they look like on. I went back and double checked what I ordered, thinking maybe there were two grays and I clicked on the wrong one - but I ordered correctly and didn’t see another one that looks like what I got that it could have been mixed up with. The shorts in the photo look like they’re made of soft, swing-y material, almost like pajamas. The fabric of the shorts I received is stiff and rough. It’s also almost a blue, and not at all the neutral gray pictured. They don’t lie flat against your body, but puff out. I’ve tried on worse shorts, and can always use these for yard work, but I don’t think I’d have looked twice at the real ones vs what I liked about the ones advertised. They aren’t sheer, they have good back coverage. I don’t understand the super heavy-duty waistband paired with an unnecessarily long and superfluous drawstring (it literally does nothing), but I guess it’s not hurting anything. Not worth the hassle of sending them back at this price point, but beware you’re not getting what you think you are.

ALMOST exactly what I was looking for... So I'd like to start off with saying these shorts were 100% what I was looking for... however, some minor changes could improve the shorts. 1) they aren't as high waisted as I hoped, the crotch inseam part makes it so the highest they can be pulled is to the belly button 2) the pockets are placed in an awkward position, kinda placed more outwards towards the thighs. This doesn't change how much I like the shorts, it just moves them from the "I love them!" category to the "I really like them!" category instead. Besides that, I find them extremely comfy and styled just the way I wanted. I weigh 190 lbs and I ordered an XL, which seems perfect for the style I was going for. I wanted them to be baggy and loose, however I do need to tie the waistband to make them stay up. If you're going for a more fitted style, order your usual size. If you're going for more of a "boyfriend short", baggy, and loose type of style- then order a size up!

Cute!! Needs a size chart Okay, so I actually really love these! However, one star deduction for not having a size chart anywhere—that drives me crazy, since clothes can vary so much within the S,M,L etc. sizing. I am usually between a small/medium in pants, size 28 roughly because I’m newly postpartum. Anyhow, I opted to get the medium because I didn’t want it to be too tight in the waist. Well… I could’ve gotten the small probably, and would’ve preferred those as I’m still losing weight. The medium DOES work for me still, it’s just a bit bigger and baggier. I think it does work with the current style of looser jeans, so I can make it work. However, as I said, a small might’ve been a bit better fitting. There isn’t a whole lot of stretch, maybe the teeny tiniest bit in the waist, but not enough to be labeled stretchy pants by any means. The makeup is 75% cotton, and the rest was viscose and polyester. They feel like medium weight denim, fairly good quality. I was actually super impressed with the color/pattern. Sometimes leopard patterns can look so incredibly cheap if not done correctly. These do not, in my opinion. They can be dressed up or down easily. Also, I am 5’7” and they go to about my ankle, a great length in my opinion. They are high waisted as well. The barrel look was the perfect amount, not overly barrel-y, but enough to clearly be barrel jeans. I found them to look quite flattering on me. Overall they felt quite comfortable! I am happy with them, I just wish I had gotten the small, but honestly I might buy the small because I like these enough to have a second pair! They also came way faster than I expected. It said it would take several weeks but came within about a week and half, so I’m super happy with that as well! Now if they could just get a size chart and actual product photos, I think these would sell out.

Cute!! Needs a size chart Okay, so I actually really love these! However, one star deduction for not having a size chart anywhere—that drives me crazy, since clothes can vary so much within the S,M,L etc. sizing. I am usually between a small/medium in pants, size 28 roughly because I’m newly postpartum. Anyhow, I opted to get the medium because I didn’t want it to be too tight in the waist. Well… I could’ve gotten the small probably, and would’ve preferred those as I’m still losing weight. The medium DOES work for me still, it’s just a bit bigger and baggier. I think it does work with the current style of looser jeans, so I can make it work. However, as I said, a small might’ve been a bit better fitting. There isn’t a whole lot of stretch, maybe the teeny tiniest bit in the waist, but not enough to be labeled stretchy pants by any means. The makeup is 75% cotton, and the rest was viscose and polyester. They feel like medium weight denim, fairly good quality. I was actually super impressed with the color/pattern. Sometimes leopard patterns can look so incredibly cheap if not done correctly. These do not, in my opinion. They can be dressed up or down easily. Also, I am 5’7” and they go to about my ankle, a great length in my opinion. They are high waisted as well. Nice fit, very comfortable These jeans might be the most comfortable jeans in my closet. I'm typically a small, with narrow hips and for me the fit around the waist and hips is perfect. They are stretchy so they will also work for someone with wider hips. The legs are consistently wide from the top of the thighs to the ankles. In my opinion, there are 2 things that could make these jeans really special from a style perspective. One is adding a little more width to the legs and the other is to make them slightly longer. I'm 5'4" and they are the perfect length, but with no room to shrink up. They will be pretty short on anyone 5'5" and over. But, some love that style so it's a preference thing I guess. The added width would give them a more unique look. But, they are great, everyday jeans that I'll wear during the day to run errands or to go hang at a friend's house. When I get home, they are so comfortable I don't feel compelled to rip them off and throw on yoga pants, like some other jeans I have. So, overall, I'm very happy with them, but wouldn't put them in "my favorites" category from a style perspective.

Ran a tiny bit larger than the size chart For reference, I'm 5'4, 165 lbs and normally wear a 12 in jeans, 12/14 in dresses or L/XL for a comfortable fit. Based on the size chart I was hesitant to order the the 12 because my waist is more of a 32 inch. The 14 I ordered fits VERY comfortably and almost a tiny bit loose. Length is just right for me. They should be perfect after a wash/dry with the cotton/poly/spandex content. I chose this wide leg style to add to my 'vintage look' wardrobe for my background acting gigs. Tapered and skinny jeans are so popular it's surprising to even find this type of relaxed look. The workmanship is superb. Nice styling with the top stitched faux front seam from knee to hem, back pockets and belt loops. A touch of acid wash look so they're a bit faded. I think I'm in love.

I like them, but they run a little large for Hudson Nicos As an elder millennial, I'm super stuck on the skinny jeans trend and having trouble getting into the straight leg style that everyone is into right now. However, I'm a huge fan of Hudson jeans, specifically the Nico style, so I was really excited to try these. And I do like them more than expected with a few caveats. They definitely run a little big when compared to other Nico jeans I have. I have several pairs of Nico Super Skinny jeans in size 25 that fit perfectly, so that's what I got these pants in, too. And while they fit OK, they're definitely large in the waist and I think I could have comfortably sized down. I'm 5'3, 100 lbs for reference. As you can probably see from my photos and the listing photos, the jeans are also quite a bit longer on me than on the model. Just a couple things to keep in mind when you're trying to decide what size to purchase! I'm still trying to feel totally comfortable in a straight leg jean, but these are helping me get there- they're super comfortable and relaxed (much more so than skinny jeans!) and they feel like a high-quality jean. If you're a fan of Hudson and looking for a good straight leg jean, I definitely recommend these... But you may want to size down from your other Hudsons!

Meh Ok so I bought these brown heart jeans for a Valentine’s Day show I’m going to and I saw the review another person posted and she had them on and they looked cute on her!!! She also said you could size down which I didn’t. I ordered a medium (I’m 5’8, 135 lbs) which is my normal size in everything and they JUST fit me. I have hips and long legs so the medium is a little tight in the hips (but still wearable) and a little too short in the length as these are boot cut so they’re supposed to hang longer. I actually might have needed a size UP!!! I cut the hem out of the bottom to try to gain some length but they’re still slightly too short for my liking. The other thing that pissed me off is that I bought them for $19.99 and then the next day they were $14.99. I’d say they’re worth $14.99 but had I tried them on in a store I wouldn’t have bought them for $20.

Very Comfortable I kept eyeing this jumpsuit and was excited to see it went on sale so I bought it right away. I’m 5’7” about 220lbs and 40D and carry most of my weight in my belly. The XXL is a perfect fit. The fabric is thin so it’s a great summer outfit. The legs are wide and flowy so super comfortable. It’s more comfortable than wearing pajamas. I WFH so it will certainly be one of my go to outfits during the week. The top area is more comfortable than I thought it would be considering it’s a scrunchy material. I’m not wearing a bra in the photos so I’d say you don’t really need one but I do recommend wearing nipple covers only because the scrunch material irritates that area. If you are wearing it for a short period of time then you can probably bypass the nip covers. At my height, the crotch is at the perfect area - not too short and not too long. The pants are an inch or two too long but I plan on mostly wearing this around the house but a platform sandal will fix that for you. The mustard color is more of a burnt orange than mustard. Doesn’t look like the color advertised but I’m completely in love and will be buying it again in black.

Size discrepancy between colors. Otherwise great skirt I bought the skirt in blue gradient and also in green, both in the large sizes. The large blue gradient one fits like a US 14, and the large green one fits like a 12. I am 5'7 approximately 205 lb, and the large blue gradient was snug but very flattering. That means that I, unfortunately, will return the large green skirt, which is probably 1-1/2 to 2 inches smaller in circumference. I'm not sure if all green skirts are off in sizing (or of the blue is off) or if it's just this one, but it was a disappointment nonetheless. Otherwise, the fabric is medium weight (i.e. won't show off every texture point from your body underneath) and has some stretch-- very flattering. The line from waist to hip will accommodate someone with a small waist and curves. The waist sits at the true waist and is not a drop waist garment.

Comfortable simple skirt Lightweight denim fabric. There is a little elastic piece in the back which gives the waist a comfortable fit. I am 5’9” and was surprised how long this was on me. I imagine for someone a little shorter it would go down to the ground.

Well made, comfortable, versatile skirt This denim skirt is a classic design, with a fly front, button. belt loops and pockets. The waistline has elastic in the back. The skirt is a nice weight and came out of the package ready to wear. It is a nice even blue and fairly soft. It has an amazing amount of stretch and is relatively long which makes it very comfortable to wear. It lends itself to casual wear or can be dressed up with a nice blouse, belt, blazer and some heeled boots. It washes well with no wrinkling or fading. I was unsure whether to order a medium or a small, (I am 5'4" and 140 lbs) and probably could have ordered a small, but with a belt the medium fits well and looks attractive. I give this skirt high marks for comfort, fit, craftmanship and versatility of wear.

Kind of Snug I think this dress is a great throwback. It has that classic western style that put Wrangler on the map. The snaps have a pearlized look that adds femininity to the design. The front pockets are functional. The sleeve length is generous. The rounded hem hit me several inches above the knee. It was just shy of being too short for me. I got the large expecting it to be a little loose from the waist down since I'm busty. The fit overall was a bit snug. I got the snaps done, but there was major gaping across the hips and bust. I would feel slightly self-conscious wearing the large. I'd definitely need to size up. For fit reference, I'm 5'6", 162lbs and a 34G bra size. The X-Large would have been ideal for me. Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided makes your decision a little easier.

Too wide in hips and waist My daughter and I were debating whether to order these in my daughter’s regular size small or go down a size and order x-small. She is 5’5” 115 lbs and wears size small in jeans. According to the included chart these numbers put her in size small. However, her hip and waist measurements put her in size x-small according to the chart. Ultimately we decided to get size small because we have a couple of other pairs of jeans from this brand and size small in those fits her great. Unfortunately, these pants are too big for her in the waist and the hips. In the pictures I took the frontal view looks great but make sure to look at the back view picture too, so you can see how my daughter tightened them with a shoe lace to make them wearable. The fabric content according to the tag is 65% cotton 20% polyester 15% spandex. I’m not a fabric specialist by any means but 15% spandex? I have never seen jeans with such a high spandex content. And wouldn’t that make them very, very stretchy? These jeans however are not very stretchy. They are definitely not all cotton because they are soft for denim and have some give but I would not describe them as stretchy denim. All in all, the style is trendy, the workmanship is good, but size wise I wish they were running more true to the standard size small jeans.

Modified Mom Jeans - Black I am 5’5” and weigh 127 lbs. I bought the size 8 in black which fit true to size. At 72 years of age I wasn’t sure if this style would be flattering on me but decided to try them anyway. The last time I wore “mom jeans” was in the 1970’s! I didn’t notice any offensive odor like some of the other people noted in their reviews. They fit snug but will need to be hemmed up about 1.5”. I have not removed the tags or washed them yet but I don’t believe they will shrink. They have plenty of sideways horizontal stretch and they sit right at the waistline. I would buy these again, maybe in a darker blue jean color.

Love these Jeans! I love these jeans. They are a great fit, super comfortable. Not constricting, easy to move in. They are stretch denim. I ordered size 10. I am 5’3” tall 150 pounds, 41-31-41. These jeans have just enough stretch so that I could go down a size. They are very long. I am wearing 4 inch heels in the pictures. If I decide to hem the jeans it should not be too difficult as the hem is a 1 inch hem sewn all around the leg. They are supposed to have tummy control. I don’t think they do. To be tummy control they need a control panel sewn from the pockets to the zipper. Other than this oversight, they are terrific jeans, I love the style, the softness of the fabric and the high waist with wide, but not baggy legs.I took off one star because they are so long and the missing panel in the tummy area.

Fine, but not my favorite pants. Usually I don't go for high wasted pants; these definitely have some mom-jeans energy, but that's more of a style preference thing. They do fit fine, and button over my belly button. I'm 5'7", 135 lbs, and wear size sm/size 4. They are comfortable and don't pinch anywhere. They're a bit stinky. I washed them with like-colors, and they definitely lost some of their color, and still carry their manufacturing odor. It's no worse than the smell of new Levi's, and I'm hoping it'll be gone after a few more washes. I'm a bit sensitive to that kind of thing. The fabric is comfy. It feels like real jeans, but has a lot of elasticity which makes movement really easy. They're a bit plain for my usual style, but aside from the odor, they seem to be well-made, and a reasonable style. If you love the high-waisted look, I think you'll be pleased with these. They're a reasonable value, and very comfortable.

It's no worse than the smell of new Levi's, and I'm hoping it'll be gone after a few more washes. I'm a bit sensitive to that kind of thing. The fabric is comfy. It feels like real jeans, but has a lot of elasticity which makes movement really easy. They're a bit plain for my usual style, but aside from the odor, they seem to be well-made, and a reasonable style. If you love the high-waisted look, I think you'll be pleased with these. They're a reasonable value, and very comfortable.

LOVE THESE JEANS I'm 5'2", 127 lbs. athletic build and got size 8. The 6 would have been good too but I like a looser fit. These jeans are GREAT, and are the first high waisted jeans I've liked (other high waists I've tried are stretchy through the leg, but the waist is too tight). The moment I slipped them on, you can feel that they are mostly cotton with enough viscose/spandex stretch. They got the right combination of materials here. Because of my height, they are longer but I wear them with 3 inch and up heel sandals and they make my legs look nice and long. I was able to kick off my heels and play with my daughter at two separate picnics and the stretch and fit makes them super comfortable. I'm thinking of what other color to buy in size 6, for those days I'm walking around with just my partner.

The jeans are a great quality super thick, beautiful color, but also super tight. Maybe I should’ve went over the reviews a little bit better as some people did say they did go up a size. For me I probably should’ve went up two sizes. In my mind I always think I’m a size 10 maybe a size 12, with these pants I probably should’ve gotten a 14. They do go super high all the way up to the small part of your waist so the part around your waist has no give no stretch. The part around your thighs your booty your legs is super stretchy, so it was hard to think what size only because I didn’t want around my legs or my booty to be super big. They are a wide legged jeans which isn’t my favorite but I honestly don’t mind these. Especially for the winter or colder months with some cute boots or maybe some UGGs. I would’ve liked for the waste part maybe to stretch a little bit more to provide a little bit more comfort. As I said they do go super high up to the smaller part of your waist almost right under your rib cage so they are super tight and there’s no give up there. I may wear these once or twice I’m kind of a little scared to wash them. I don’t want them to get smaller or tighter. I am 5‘,4“ and for how high they go they were right at my ankle so if you are taller and they do go that high up your waist they’re gonna be very short. I feel a little 50-50 on these. Like I said the gene the stretch is very nice. Nice color, but I find them too tight maybe a little too uncomfortable for a lot of wear. Maybe for a nice outing or date I can totally wear these. I’m not very happy of how high they go only because I’m not very thin and I feel like that smaller part of your stomach at the bottom Does show a lot so a longer sweater or a longer jacket will definitely be my choice.

Stylish, quality and fit. I love this jeans. It’s stylish and on trend. The fit is loose and comfortable. It’s loose from the hip down, however the waist area fits snug even though it has an elastic waistband. I ordered a size large with a 35” waist. I feel if I had ordered a size up for a more loose fit in the waist area then the rest of the jeans would be extremely loose and awkward. The quality and craftsmanship is excellent. The fabric is thick and not flimsy. The zipper and button is functional. It features pockets in the back, and deep pockets in the front, as well as a drawstring belt. The color and design is lovely. Overall this is a good quality jeans and I feel it’s worth the value.

Not Disappointed - Value=Price I’m currently: 30 weeks - 5’5” - 152lbs. Have mainly gained weight in belly/boobs not so much hips/legs/butt. My impressions: opening the package, yes they have a smell. I guarantee this goes away once washed. Look: I received what is modeled regarding the color and distressing. Size: maybe a little bit long, but I like that I can wear them as a full pant or cuffed (pictured both). With how and where I carry weight a Medium felt true to size for me. Fit: I didn’t look at the size chart but what I can say is that the belly band sits a little high for me and my liking. I prefer it either completely under or over my belly. I am able to fold just the front elastic down and that sits perfectly under my belly. The legs are just a little loose - but that’s how I like my jeans to fit. Stretch: the right amount of elastic. I’m 95% positive that I’ll be able to wear these for the remainder of my pregnancy. Overall: I’m not disappointed in purchasing these jeans. I feel that the price matches the quality/value of the pants.

PT pregnant w/ 4th I am a personal trainer, 27 weeks pregnant with my 4th. All my previous maternity pants have holes and look old. I am also getting larger faster being it my 4th. So i needed comfortable, flattering, and reliable new pants. I am 5'3" and pre pregnancy i am 110-118lbs at my fittest. Now i am at 140lbs and these pants fit like a glove and feels like they will till the end. They check all the boxes for both work and home wear. From chasing my 3 little boys around, resting, to working with clients in the gym. I prefer cotton as a fabric because it is more comfy and warm to me but these feel close to the one pair of Atheleta pants gifted to me and i am definitely satisfied. Maternity pants are HARD to shop for. I hate when the crotch area bags or the belly band is uncomfortable or falls down, and so many are so thin... Not this one. I very MUCH RECOMMEND this brand to all expecting mamas. Will buy from them again.

Womens imitation leather belt We ordered this faux leather belt from QiqiLuxi and it was priced at $10 at the time. The quality of the fake leather is about average, your basic inexpensive material. The buckle and belt tip where actually heavier duty than I expected and look pretty good. The belt material, 3 stars, the simple inexpensive buckle, 5 stars. The posting itself seems misleading as this is a womens belt, it is much to thin to be considered a mens belt. The size also suggests it is good for up to 38" waists... the limit is closer it 33". The miss has 38" hips and no way this will work low low cut jeans, it was in the furtherst hole with a set of very high waisted jeans (on the miss with a 32" waist). I have 4 stars as the buckle and belt cap are nice for the price, but it could of got 3 stars as the belt itself is cheep material and shorter than expected.

Is it really unisex? It's much skinnier than a standard men's belt. The belt material has a squishy feel when you squeeze it. It's definitely fake leather, but it doesn't look bad; thus, convincing enough to pass as real leather if you don't touch it. Some observations: 1. The belt came in one size (in length). I didn't see any options to choose sizes when you placed your order. The farthest hole accommodated a 33" waist. Lucky for me, it fitted me okay, but it might not be the same for you if your waist is over 33". 2. The belt is a bit skinny (in width) for a men's belt. I have about 10 or so belts in my closet and this belt is skinnier than every one of them. All of the Seller's pictures depicted women wearing this belt. Hmmm... 3. The overall design looked great. The stitching looked okay. The fake leather looked slightly distressed so its should blended in, hopefully, when there is more wear and tear.

Weird Sizing - Strange Fit Generally, I wear size 20 jean. Size 22 will fit me if I sew darts in the back to tighten up the waist. I read through comments on this product, and ordered a size up because so many people said these run small. So, I ordered 3X. The legs fit my thick legs, though snug, and length was perfect for my 5’4” frame. (I would consider the length perfect for a petite person.). The waist though… IT WAS HUGE. It would fit me if I were 6 months pregnant. The styling of this jean is so cute, and I like the lightweight denim for summer. Even so, I will return these, because if I keep, I’ll need to tailor the waist substantially.
Love em' These jeans fit perfect for me. I ordered a size large. I'm 5'6 180 pounds. They have some stretch to them as well. Good jean quality.

Very long, good style I've been looking for a decent pair of ladies cargo jeans when I came across these. They don't fit me the way it looks on the models but I'll probably still keep. For starters, they're really long. I am 5'1 and petite. After reading many reviews about these being too small, and even referencing the size chart, I bought a medium. The size small was not on prime. The pants are definitely a size too big on my waist. I like the loose fit, but I may need to use a belt. My waist is a 27 inch, and I usually wear a size 4. I washed and dried hoping they'd shrink a bit more but they did not. Tucking in a shirt helps make them fit better as well. Order your normal size. When the small comes back on prime I'll definitely try them.
