As cute as they are in the picture I’ve had my eye on these jeans for a while. I hesitate buying jeans without trying them on though, because it’s a hit or miss if they fit or not. I’m so glad I ordered them. I love these jeans!! The quality is great, they don’t feel like cheap or generic denim. They have good stretch too, and comfortable enough to move around in. The inseam is pretty long though. Even with 2-3 inch heels I still have to fold them at the bottom. Then again, I am only 5’2” lol. I like how it has the drawstring at the bottom, bc I was able to pull them a little tighter to prevent from dragging too much when I’m wearing my platform Chucks. In the picture I’m wearing chunky 3 in heels and with it rolled up once, they’re the perfect length. I followed the size chart and bought the right size (XL) according to the size chart. They fit perfect. For reference, I have a 33.5 in waist, curvy hips, I have some booty back there lol and thick thighs. And they fit like a glove on me. I felt like they hugged me in all the right spots. If you want the loose, baggy look, I would size up. But I love these jeans so much and I feel like they can be dressed up or dressed down. They remind me of the jeans we wore in high school, and I love the flap pockets on the butt. It’s more flattering for your booty. Only reason I gave 4 stars is because they are super long. And with the drawstring, can’t really hem them. It would be awesome if made these with different inseam lengths. Still love the jeans either way, these jeans are gonna be a part of my regular rotation.

10/10 I'm a person that HATES tight jeans because the material is so uncomfortable and itchy that when I wear MOST skinny jeans I can't wait to go home and take them off. I was expecting these jeans to be the same since they were from amazon but when I put them on I was so suprised that the material was soft quality and didn't make me dread wearing them. I wore them out and my BF when he saw the jeans on me had his eyes practically pop out of his head. These jeans hug every inch of my curves and they are so comfortable to wear for long hours. I could sleep in them and coming from ME that says alot. I will be purchasing more of these colors and others from here. I don't know how these will old up and if the quality of the fabric will remain the same after wash so I plan to wash on cold and hang dry these jeans to avoide any issues. I love these jeans so much. On my way to buy more pairs. BTW: The top I am wearing is a black turtle neck bodysuit jumpsuit (long sleeves) that I also purchased here on amazon. Both pieces plus a pair of black flats really put me together like I said when I took my jacket off my boyfriend's eyes popped out of his sockets because the jeans and the turtle neck bodysuit hug my whole body. MEASUREMENTS: I'm 5'3 148 pounds and I bought a size 11 and it fits perfectly. A size 13 would have been to large and not as flattering. size 11 is not to big still plenty of stretch to material to not hug your body tight. I normally wear a sieze 10 in jeans so I went up one size. Best decision ever. GET THESE JEANS!!!

Comfortable! I got a size 7, but I probably could’ve gone another size up since it’s tight around the waist. I do have a scar on my stomach, but the button doesn’t pinch it so it’s not painful (I don’t know my hip size, but I’m around 120 lbs), but for someone else who has bigger hips or stomach, I would recommend going 2 sizes up. Keep in mind the waistline will be the tightest part compared to the rest of the jeans. Overall, the pants are actually fairly comfortable and the material isn’t scratchy at all. It’s somewhat stretchy. Some jeans can be irritating if you don’t shave your legs, but this pair is comfy regardless if you shave or not! It’s only been one day, and the zipper keeps riding down when moving around. I don’t know if it’s because the waistline of the jeans is too small for me or if it’s just a general issue, but it isn’t a big deal. Jeans are a big long too, since I’m 5’3 and it kind of scrunches up around the ankles, but that’s an easy fix! I don’t have hardly any curves and my butt is flat as can be, but the pants did give me some of a figure (not by much haha!). It also didn’t slide down while bending, so that’s a plus. I never wore high waist jeans before, but this might turn me over.

Magical Mom Jeans make me look more Shapely & Curvaceous Stretchy, Soft, Well Made I must say, I am very pleased I bought these magical mom jeans in black and medium wash. My waist is 25.5", 33.5" hips. I ordered size 3. They fit beautifully. I have smallish hips and a wider waist so I was worried that the waist band would be too tight. It is snug, but it does have some stretch, so I think as long as I don't gain weight around my waist, they should be comfy. I have only tried these on so far, I haven't actually worn them anywhere or washed them. They are soft and stretchy. I thought the black might be more crispy and less stretchy, but they are about the same. The fit on both pair was identical. The jeans really make the most of my small butt, and they make my waist look smaller, which gives the illusion of more of a heart-shape, when in reality, my waist to hip transition is pretty pretty straight. I can do a deep squat in these jeans without having to adjust them. In the pictures I am wearing them with panties that create lines. I don't want these to shrink, so I will probably wash in cold and hang dry. The fabric looks like it has good structure and is unlikely to stretch out or sag, but time will tell. I paid less than $30 each, which I think is a great price for what I received. Plus I ordered them day before yesterday and they arrived this morning. Can't beat that!

Highly recommend! Super retro I have been looking for high waisted jeans that were high enough to cover my belly button, I have some cute crop tops that I wanted to wear without exposing my stomach. I searched the net and these were the only review pics sent in that showed the jeans covering the belly button. They are more like jeggings than jeans which I love because they have great stretch, soft, and comfy, yet they look like jeans. You get the best of both worlds. I'm 5'2 120lbs I got the size 3. I could tell they were long from other reviews so I planned to roll them up anyway giving them more of Vintage look which was what I was going for. Originally I was going to go up a size to a 5 but after reading the reviews I figured a 3 would work best and it did. They looked too small when I opened the bag but They had great stretch and fit me just the way I wanted them to. I highly recommend these and am ordering more. I'm not sure how long they will last as I just wore once but honestly I will buy again even if they last only through out the year. I'd gladly buy a pair year, as long as they didn't get crazy expensive. I wouldn't normally spend $30 on jeans from online but since I couldn't find any super high waisted jeans Anywhere else I thought to give these a try

Hold the phone... Hold the door... Hold my purse because I'm about to buy 10 pairs. I'm 5'2 about 115-125 lbs with a mom pouch I've been looking to hide so I can enjoy my last year of my 20s with crop tops and care free style. I was super worried about their junior sizing method (I got a size 5) and that buying a pant online can be a true gamble. All I can say is, BUY IT! Worst case, return and get a bigger size because these jeans will be a staple for me. They fit like a jegging with structure. They have fading on the back that accentuate dat a**. When I pulled the pants out of the box I was wondering if they mailed me toddler pants or if I was duped by the all hated chinese sizing guide. Naw, they stretched where the curves were and I didnt have to struggle to button because of the right amount of give that they have. The wash on it is also super flattering for night or day. Ignore my messy room, the holidays have caused havoc on our whole house. I'm in love. Get you some.

May not need to size up. I am 5’9 - 137 - 25.5-26 inch waist and 36.5 hip and the size 5 were a little too loose. I’m so sad bc they are so cute so I’m exchanging them for 3. I like a tight fit and there was so much room in the hips and stomach area for the size 5. Should have gone with 3’s. They are tight in the calves but mine are like jokingly tiny so I could see how that may be a problem for someone else. Update: (picture included) I like the 3’s better. I probably could have gone with 1’s for reference. May pick up 1s later but the 3s are nice. I’m pretty happy with the butt but it could be slightly more flattering with the back pockets in my opinion but great for me to wear these as they’re extremely comfy and I’m a leggings or skirt/dress person. I’m 5 months postpartum so I haven’t been quite sure about what size I am. I love that this product has free returns because the original size I ordered was too tight (i originally ordered size 9 but returned them for size 11). Im currently 157 pounds with most of my weight being in my thighs and butt. Size 11 fits just right (pics attached). Size 13 may fit even better, but my goal is to loose some more weight so Im keeping the 11. The pants feel comfty (not itchy or stiff). I can bend in them now that theyve fitted to my body. In the beginning it def felt tighter but have to give it time to form fit. I love that they are high waisted as they help shirts fit much better bc they cover my tummy weight. The button is just on my belly button. The pants have two long back pockets (fits my iphone). I want to get the other colors. I would def recommend.

These are my go to jeans Ever since I started working in an office I needed high waisted jeans that make me look beautiful but also fit me well and are comfortable. Well theseee are it! Like Jeggings. Comfortable. Dark denim size 3 fits 5'2" 103LBs I am usually a junior jeans size 1, 3, or even 0 depending on the brand. The 3 fits comfortably. These Are The Cutest!!!! Okay, my quick review photos definitely do not do these justice, but I did wear them out recently and I got a bunch of compliments! The fit is great, the color is the perfect shade of burnt / rust orange, and they are super comfortable and fun! I got a size large because based on the size chart I probably could have gotten a medium but I wanted them a little on the looser side and I think they're perfect. For reference, I am 5'6" and currently around 160 lbs. Of course the straps are adjustable and it has the buttons on the side to help get them on over the hips, although I can get them on and off easily without undoing any of the buttons. Ooh also they have a hidden inner button on either hip so you can expand the waist another couple inches if you need, which is a nice feature! The overalls themselves feel very well made with sturdy buttons and seams. The fabric is maybe a little thinner than your standard jean denim, but I personally like that they are a little lighter. They feel more like a twill pant material than true thick denim, but they still feel like decent quality, and most importantly they are comfortable! I honestly feel like these will become my go-to outfit this fall and I can't wait to wear them again and again!

Sturdy, trendy belt for a great price I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of this belt, the strap is sturdy and the buckle is comparable to the ones I've seen at Urban Outfitters or Topshop. I have a 25" waist and bought the 24"-28" belt. The fit is quite true to size. In addition, the width of the belt is about 1 inch, which might be a tight fit if your belt loops are on the smaller side. I've worn this belt maybe 10+ times and have noticed minimal scuffing. Overall, it's a great quality belt for a decently inexpensive price.

I am so happy with the quality and fit of these fleece jeans! They are thick and warm. I live in Ontario and took them for a walk in - 6 (feels like -11, celsius) and they kept me toastie warm. I'm 5'7 and 54kg and followed the size chart - purchased size 6 as recommended (I'm normally a size 4) and it's true to size. Style in black is high waisted which is exactly what I want! The butt and waist area is only slightly looser than I'm used to (most jeans are tight on my bum) but this is not noticeable and the pants do not slide down. I really want to order more in different colours but judging by other photos they appear to fit mid to low waist. Very happy with this purchase though. I have not machine washed it yet but expect placing it in a washing bag (fleece on inside) on cold gentle wash should keep it safe. I will update if it does not wash well.

Cute but what's up with the rivets falling off I wore these for 90 minutes before the first rivet came off! I didn't realize they are just leggings with a denim look until I received them... they have a stretch waistband, pull-on style, with no zipper. The back pockets are functional but the front ones are not. I am 5'6 and 132 lbs, I ordered the small/medium capris in navy and they fit very well. The “one size” was ok... smaller than other one size pants I’ve tried but still not small enough. I’m 5.7 and 120 lb and they were baggy on the knees and ankles.

READ BEFORE YOU BUY... then buy!!!!!! :-) Order way up. I ordered a 1x/2x, here's why. I read many of the reviews carefully and referenced their size chart. I was reading... too small, annoying stripes, etc. That's because these are small. They are super stretchy, but have a snug waistband. They are surprisingly slimming... if you order right. I am 5'5" and 150 and a 3-c-section belly. I usually wear size medium or size 8-10. This is why I ordered the 1\2x and they fit absolutely comfy and amazing. I am not the type that orders small and tries to stretch to the max. I'd rather order big and be extremely comfortable. These have no negatives on any part of my body for ordering big and no overly stretchy lines. I just think their size chart and measurements are like Japanese and European that run extremely small and make us feel fat. Lol. I hope this helps. I will absolutely order more colors and in the bigger size.

Super comfy and accurate sizing! I was looking for a comfy skort I could wear in the summer and this delivered! I was prepared to be underwhelmed because it had no reviews when I ordered it lol. It definitely exceeded expectations! I'm 5'5, 122lbs and usually around 25in for bottoms. The sizing chart was very accurate and the small fits well! I probably could have gone with an XS for a more snug fit, but I wanted a little room for a more relaxed fit. This is a very comfortable, slightly oversized feel, like a boyfriend jean. The pockets are very deep and the material is soft. Went in the washer and dryer with no problems. Would highly recommend!

Replacing my discontinued jeans for these. I want to be as detailed as possible. Firstly as I write this it's more of first impressions than an actual review. I'm 5'9" and currently weigh 243lbs. I ordered these pants based off of Amazon's recommendations in size 18. These pants just fit. And I don't mean that in a bad way. They are not too big or too tight anywhere. They just fit me. Lol. I got some junk in the trunk. I struggle with chub rub and wide calves. Again nothing feels constrained. The material has that rough texture like normal jeans. It's made with 3% spandex, which surprisingly gives a lot of stretch overall to the pants. My usual go to pair of high waisted black jeans have been Old Navy's Rockstar super skinny high rise 24/7 sculpt pants in size 14 regular. These pants are a pretty close match. Those jeans have been my everything. I work all day in those pants and live in those pants. And I can't get them anymore. That's how I ended up here trying these pants. I could probably size down to a 16 for a more fitted pair. But I'm not going to return these ones. They are high rise for sure. If you need pants to work in like me these will do. I can bend/squat down in these and not fear I'm revealing my backside. I did see a review before ordering these about the back pockets being more outward/on the side. And they might be further outward than most jeans. But that's okay with me. I carry my phone in my back pocket so having the pockets more outward prevents me from crushing my phone. Overall, I'm happy with the purchase and the first impressions. I don't know how well they will hold up with lots of use. I go through jeans and shoes frequently. I do a lot walking/bending/squating etc at work. And maintain an active lifestyle outside of work too. So these pants will get a lot of work out of them. If anything major happens I will update this review. Hope this helps.

Size chart wrong but pants are great my measurements are 37 waist 52 hips. According to their size chart, my waist would fit in a size 20 plus pants and my hips wouldn’t fit in any Size period. I had to get a belt to keep the pants up cause they’d slowly fall down. But since I am a chubby girl, when I sit I kinda splooge out so I’m def glad I have some waist room. Great jeans for plus size gal! I purchased these jeans not really knowing what to expect, but I’m glad I did! For men too! I love these pants! I have never found a pair of men's jeans that fit right. Most mainstream men's skinny jeans are too baggy or too stiff. And most non mainstream men's skinny jeans are overpriced and too cramped where it matters. But these are perfect! I love the length, the high waist (for extra room for your jonson), the stretchy-ness, the price, and the style! For reference I am 5'8", 130lbs, 28" waist, and I fit a size 5 perfectly. At first I bought the zipper fly pair and have worn them for a while. I wanted a second pair since they ended up becoming my favorite pair of jeans, so I decided to buy another. Except they only had the button fly jeans available. I am now a button fly guy. I like them a lot. They look better than zipper fly IMO. If there was one thing I would critique it would be the ability for the jeans to hold shape after a few days. Day one they fit perfectly, day two a little more lose but not bad, day three and you might need a belt, day four and they are for sure going to sag without a belt. Wash them up and they fit perfectly again. It's not that bad since four days and the pants should probably be washed anyway. I guess the last critique is that they attract a LOT of fur. If you have cats or dogs, beware! Buy some lint rollers.

Tighter waist/no gap For reference, I'm 5'3", 140ish lbs, usually a size 6 and ordered these shorts in a medium. They are a great length, and I love how high-waisted they are. I think they do have a slightly tighter waist than many other shorts out there, but keep in mind that can be really good for girls who sometimes struggle with gapping in the back since these have more of that curvier fit! They feel like a pretty good denim with a little bit of stretch. If you want, you can also roll them up once or twice or cut the strings if you don't like them as much (although on mine it did seem like the inside was a slightly darker black that you can tell when you roll it). These will be great for summer!

Love ❤️ These shorts are the perfect denim shorts for the summer. They are so comfortable and have a decent amount of stretch. They are a thinner denim, which is perfect for the summer. One of the other reviewers mentioned that they’re thin as if that were a bad thing. I’m not sure why anyone would want to wear thick denim in the middle of summer. I live in Florida and I prefer light clothing when it gets hot out. They are a substantial denim, not cheap. I ordered a size up because I didn’t want them to be tight. I’m glad I did. I’m usually a size 2, but ordered a size 4 based on another review I saw/ read. She has almost the same stats as me and she ordered a size 2. I think they look great on her, but I definitely wanted a loose fit. I’m 5’4” and weigh 133 lbs. I definitely recommend these denim shorts!

Comfortable and versatile denim shorts Finally, I found a pair of denim shorts that I can comfortable wear year round! Generally, I avoid denims because they are difficult to fit properly and are too hot to wear in the warm climates I always live in. But, I always thought denims look good and go with just about every top. This pair of Awuka denim shorts solved my problem. The material is very stretchy and doesn't bind anywhere. The cut is modest being high waisted and with the hem length. The pockets are deep enough. The stitching is good. I did trim off the fray strings at the hem as that's not me. Importantly, the material is not hot. I machine washed and tumbled dry and it didn't shrink. The small fits me quite well. My waist is 28 inches, weight is 120 lbs and height is 5'5". I would purchase this again.

Waist not-so-elastic I wish they had fit. I sized them based on another brand of jegging. Seems like they would have been very comfortable if I could have gotten them on, but at this rate I would need a large and they probably wouldn't fit right. 5'3" 140ish lbs and I weightlift. Size small.

VERY comfortable but thin, so plan to wear them with tunics unless you have seamless thongs and nothing to hide Those jeggings are true-to-size (I'm 5'5", 125 lbs, wear size 8 pants. I ordered the Medium and it's perfect), very stretchy, well made, and extremely comfortable. They're also pretty versatile as the leg bottom can be folded out (without looking bad) to wear as capris. However, as a few customers have mentioned, they're quite thin, so plan to wear them with long shirts/tunics because every single panty line and bulge will show. Also noteworthy: The buttons up front and on the back pockets are studded with "diamonds". Unless you're into the fake-diamond look, that's yet another reason to wear those with long shirts/tunics. The choice of colors, comfort and price make them a pretty attractive option (hence the 4 stars), but keep in mind they may target to a younger, on-the-thin-side crowd...

Good for the price. Could be better. I got the plum colored pants in size medium. I am 5'6" and I typically wear a size 4-6. The length on these pants was just right. I'm unsure how I feel about the fit of the pants on me, but I'd say these are comparable to a 2-4 sized pant. I wanted these to look like skinny jeans, but feel like leggings. For the most part, they accomplish that. The fabric is thin, soft, and stretchy, which is good for physical comfort, but also means that the fabric really conforms to your body and doesn't feel like it's hiding anything. For this reason, I'd personally stick to darker colors that might not show every dimple off.

Soft & Comfy, Pricey, But Not A Great Fit For Pear-Shaped Women, I *WILL* Luncle Dry! I'm just under 5'8" and down to 195lbs at the time of my order. I lost over 60 lbs last year, from a size 22-24 to I'm not sure what size exactly. This might be important for others to know. I'm still losing slowly, but based on the measurements I took of myself before ordering (Waist 39", Hips 45", Inseam 32-34"), I optimistically ordered size 14 -- and miraculously, they fit, just a bit snug but not overly tight. I think the slight stretch to the material helped with that. The tag was so amusing that I had to share. I'm too old to deal with hand washing anything, so if it doesn't survive my machine, it's not worth having. You can bet that not only did I wash it in my machine (cold water, normal setting, with the rest of my clothes), I also tumbled it dry -- to be fair, I did follow the instructions to not luncle dry it! They came out just fine, with no noticeable shrinkage. If machine washing & drying makes them wear out faster, that's just too bad. The most I'll go out of my way for something special I rarely wear is to put it in a net bag to wash and hang to dry -- but that's only if I have the time and inclination. :) The only problem I have with them is the same as with many pants: there is a gap at my lower back since my hips & butt are bigger than my waist. I have to wear a belt with these pants to correct that. They are not really made for women with my type of curves. I'm also a notorious penny-pincher, and these pants are nearly $40, which is almost twice as much as I prefer to pay for pants. These are comfy & soft, but the material is also thin, so I don't know how long they'll last before they tear. I've only worn them a few times since I received & washed them. For this price, I hope they last a long time. If they do tear or have any problems prematurely, I will come back to update.

This will be the most HONEST REVIEW you've ever seen..... I bought the Black in size 12 and the Green in size 14, but only bcuz a Green size 12 didn't become available Until AFTER I let the seller know they were running out of sizes. However, I have tried Shrinking the Green size 14 and they did shrink a little bit. I will try to Shrink the Green some more, but I will need to Stretch the Legs while they are hot and/or Use a Steamer to Stretch the Length, bcuz I really don't want the Length to Shrink! Also, as you will see in the photos, the Black pair had Tags etc strung into a little black loop inside the back of the pants, whereas the Green pair didn't have any Tags nor a special little black loop for the Tags, so I almost kinda feel that the Green pair were a Return, but that's ok, bcuz I've since washed them... But truthfully, I'm shocked that these pants/jeans are as Amazing, Soft and Super Comfy as they are! When I first grabbed the package from being downstairs and outside at the door, it didn't feel like a package of jeans. It felt waaaaay too light for jeans and I was almost disappointed! But... I opened the package and decided to try them on anyway! Wow!! I am absolutely blown away! Not only do they Fit according to the Size Chart, (I'm 5' - 6.5" and about 145ish pounds) but they are a really nice, soft and a "comfy around the waist" pair of jeans, that I honestly think will become my Favorite pair of jeans!! Due to stomach issues, I don't usually like anything around or touching my upper tummy, but these are sooo Light and Soft that they honestly Don't bother my tummy 1 bit! I'm even been sitting and laying on the couch to check out their comfortness and they pass with flying colors.. COMFORT Beyond Belief!! And bcuz of how extremely Comfortable they are, I am now going to order a couple more pairs!I've already ordered and received the Green pair, but I'm going to get another Black pair like these ones, and I think I'll get a pair of the Darker Blue ones IF the seller has more of the Blue ones in a size 12?!? I do have 1 "Slight" issue, and it's nothing major, it's a "Me Thing" just bcuz I'm very picky when it comes to a Pants Length, I do prefer my pants to be Longer, and so, I wish these jeans were another Inch, or even preferably 2 inches Longer!! But as they are right now, they almost touch the floor at my 5' - 6.5" of height! And like I said, it's a "Me Thing", I just prefer Extra Long Pants!! However, after washing them and before hanging them up to dry, I'm going to try and Stretch the Legs out in order to Gain a little bit more Length!! And IF this doesn't work, I may have to use Hot Steam to Stretch them out a bit, bcuz Hot Steam always works when wanting to Stretch out clothing!! **I have since washed both pairs in cool water and tumbled dried them for a bit with a little bit of Heat and I was able to stretch the Length back.** Anyway, being an extremely Picky person, I personally and highly Recommend these jeans! You Won't be Disappointed!! I truly hope you Grow to Love them as much as I do, and I've only had them on for 30 minutes just to try them out... (I've now had them for 2 days and have washed both the Black and Green pair and All was good) But I do know already, that they will and have, become my FAVORITE pair of Cargo jeans... And yes, it's true that they are Not a Heavy Cotton jean, but that's why I Love them so much, bcuz with them being a lighter fabric, it makes them Easier to move around in! I can do Squats and whatever else in them, plus Sit for Hours in them and I'm still very comfortable and it's all bcuz of how Flexible they are with being a Lighter fabric/material... --Bottom Line, I have absolutely NO PROBLEM with these Cargo jeans! They are absolutely Amazing and I'm sure you will Love them as much as I do...--

Too long for my 5'6" height Despite being a size 12, I ordered these pants with wide legs in a medium. With a 34-36" waist and ample room in the thigh area, I assumed the pants would be a good fit and they were. However at 5'6", the pants are excessively long, and I would advise those shorter to be warned. In the advertisement, the material of the slacks appears to be of higher quality than it actually is, but given the summer heat, I was not bothered by the difference. The front pleat did nothing to flatter my stomach (pudge). But, the pants' deep pockets did contribute to its comfort. In general, I'm not sure if I can wear these without alterations or without the hemline touching the ground, even with heels.

Class-ayy This is a great pair of pants! Several positives... namely, I'm 5'10 & although these aren't the perfect length for me they are doable whereas the VAST majority of other pants are not. You cannot get away with rolling a too short wide leg to pull off a little cropped look, it's uhhh pretty bad. The color is great, vibrant while suitable for everyday dressy/casual wear. They are lightweight without feeling cheap, I've been able to wear them during the summer. The fit is pretty true to size, the only downside being the button hits riiight above my post babies pooch, accentuating it in a way I don't love lol but that's more of a me issue, I guess. If they had buttoned a little higher/lower, been slightly more flowy/billowy in that spot, or pleated in a way that didn't draw the attention there, that would've been nice though.

The strap I adjusted to its longest length, and it it’s my 5’4 frame at the hip when worn crossbody. When not taking this to work, I have tons more room because I don’t need the speaker or markers. I love it!!

Perfect little denim jacket Perfection! I bought this in both White and Wishful Blue. They are both a soft, comfy denim fabric with a slightly fitted shape. Not too fitted, but not that typical stiff boxy jeans jacket. At 5’2” somewhere between a 12 -14, I was pleased to find the size Large was the perfect fit. The “hem” hits me right above/at the hips, which is perfect for wearing over a spring/summer dress. I love the pockets, which are big enough to actually tuck your hands into. I like that it’s not too distressed. No rips or holes; just the frayed hem and a few “worn” spots. The wishful blue color is so pretty, It’s a nice worn blue. Not white washed. Not acid washed. Best barrel jeans *update* 10/10, absolutely LOVE these. I’ve tried MANY barrel jeans on Amazon at this point and I think these are my fav so far. Primarily because they have some stretch and are the perfect length! I’m 5’7” for reference and they are slightly cropped without being too cropped and riding too high. They are also mid rise on me, making them flattering and comfortable! I’ve found that many others are so tight and rigid in the waist but too wide everywhere else and these fit like a glove, in the most flattering way…. They fit my waist so comfortably, but clearly look like barrel jeans without being too wide and crazy looking. Also, size down!! I am typically a medium, but ordered a small since someone said they’re stretchy and these fit SO well. The only gripe I had is that they are slightly darker than pictured… but they do have the sort of acid wash/worn look to them, like the photos show, and so I am pleased with that. I’ve already put another color in my cart and will likely buy them asap… the price is also good for the quality and fit. Stoked to wear these! —an additional bonus is that they came quickly, within a couple days. They didn’t have any odd smells to them, so I am super happy! Don’t question it, get them! (They look even darker in my mirror pictures due to poor lighting, so they are a smidge lighter than that) ***update! I bought a second pair in a lighter color and I love them just the same. They’re the best. I wear them ALL the time. They’re my new everyday jean. They’re sooo comfy but so flattering. I did have to have my mom sew and fix the pockets so the lining didn’t keep showing, but it was an easy fix and they never show anymore!

Had a weird greasy feel to them, way too short on me, legs were different lengths. I am 5'7" and 160lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. I got this in a size L and they fit great around my waist, butt, and thighs. They were really short though, I know they are supposed to be cropped but they were ridiculously short on me. To add to that the two legs were shaped slightly differently and uneven, it was really odd.These were also really weird feeling. They had a greasy feel to them that left my hands feeling slippery, I ended up trying them on over leggings because I didn't want to get the residue on my legs. I have never had a pair of jeans have this kind of greasiness to them.I was really excited to try out these barrel leg jeans and very disappointed in the quality. The greasy residue that they seemed to bathed in was a huge yuck factor for me but might have washed off in the wash. The fact that they legs weren't the same length was just poor manufacturing. I didn't keep these and can't recommend them.

My New Favorite Winter Jeans Love these jeans! I've had fleece lined jeans before from LL Bean and I'd say these are definitely a contender for the savings. These came out of the packaging a little smelly, even after their first wash smelled a little yuck. But they aired out and the smell is totally gone. Overall, I definitely recommend them if you're looking for an affordable and comfortable pair of warm jeans for cool weather.

A "skinny" jean actually cut to fit a curvy body! Given that I am curvy (a pretty traditional pear shape with a smaller waist and larger hips, bottom, and thighs), I have always been wary of "skinny" jeans and have tended to stick with more boot-cut styles. After the rave reviews about these jeans for curvy women, I decided to try them. I am most often a size 8 in jeans, but I got these in a 10, as I thought that I would need the extra size due to the "skinny" cut.These jeans are a dark rinse with a slight whiskering. They are described as having a "flex and flatter fabric," and I would say that this is true--there is a nice stretch to them, and they comfortable hugged my curves. The length was perfect for me (5'4.5"), just past the ankles. I do think a size 10 was the correct size for me in this style, although as you can see from my photos, I did still have a slight gap to the waistband at the back. However, since these jeans have a hidden elasticity to the waist, the waistband did hug my body, and the small gap was barely noticeable. The waistband hit just above my hip bones and right at my belly button. These jeans were also flattering to the booty, with a nice placement to the pockets plus a curved yoke/shaping darts.I have machined washed/dried these jeans, and they seemed to turn out well with no shrinkage. Overall, these are definitely a nice pair of jeans for the curvy woman, and I would recommend me them, especially for those who have a bit less of a waist-to-hip ratio than I do.1/11/23 UPDATE:I still have these and they have become my favorite jeans! I've gained a little weight with menopause, so either that or the jeans shrinking slightly have helped them to fit better. They are comfortably stretchy and feel just right.

Great fit! I was hesitant to purchase jeans online because I wasn't sure what I would do if they didn't fit. It's one thing to purchase another pair of jeans when you already have a pair and you know your size. It's completely different to purchase a pair blind without having a reference point. So, I looked at the reviews and I saw where a woman who is 5'3" says they fit her well. She said they fit "perfect". I'm 5'2' so I was SOLD because of her review. I mean, the jeans look great on the model, but I wasn't sure they'd look or feel great on me, especially since I'm short. Well, blow me over with a feather! i am so excited I pulled the trigger. The color is beautiful. They're a deep, dark blue. The length is perfect. Like I said, I'm an inch shorter than the other woman and that 1 inch does not make them go from the perfect length to too long. They fit well in the stomach. I have a stomach. I birthed two children and I'm fat. Unless I pay a doctor in Mexico to fix this, that stomach and I will be best friends until I die. They are not too tight. They stretch to accommodate my belly well. I usually wear a 12 which fit my belly well, but are too big in the legs. They sag, and I'm not a 20 year old boy who sags on purpose. i just don't have enough going on to fill out the pants. i chose a 10. It was available and I'm losing weight by eating keto. Although I've lost 6 or 7 pounds and these fit GREAT, they would fit better if I lost another 5 pounds. I don't have a great shaped lower (or upper) body and they're not magic, but they look good and feel good. I don't know about sewing, but whatever "darts" they added to enhance my shape did a pretty decent job.

Good looking skinny jeans with weak seams. While these are very comfortable and fit nicely, I did find a rip along the outside seam after wearing only once. After looking at the seams closely, I can see that it is not sewn with strong thread and will have the propensity to come undone with little pressure. I only wore the pants for an hour and had been sitting in them when I noticed the rip along the seam. I'm going to have a tailor fix the rip and reinforce the seams as I really do like the look and the fit of the pants. Other than this problem, I love them. They wash well and the material has just the right amount of stretch. Being that I only have worn them for an hour, I can't say whether they stretch out more than they should over the hours of wearing them, but after I get them fixed, I will update the review with how they hold up. I wash them in cold, gentle cycle, using Woolite Dark (since they are black jeans). I then lay them to dry. While I really do like the pants, I'd probably think twice about purchasing this brand again only because of the ripped seam. I am 5'4" and 155 pounds. The size 10 short fit nicely and came down to just below my ankle. I really like the panel in the front of the jeans that help keep my tummy in a bit (see picture).

Truly Sculpting! The most comfortable jeans ! I do not like trying on pants or jeans I avoid it at all costs they are very tricky to fit on my curvy very short 61 year old body. However these were a winner ! I purchased the F'AB BODY SCULPT SKINNY style. These jeans have lots of stretch, the waistband in particular is so comfortable and it is high enough to cover muffin tops. They are intended to be ankle length but I have to hem up about 2" as I am 5'2". The sculpting darts and elastic panels really do work ! I am size 8 but originally purchased size 10 based on some reviews and I was nervous that an 8 would be too tight. The 10 fit but after wearing a few days the waistband loosened up and I was constantly hiking them up. I switched that out for a size 8 . I recommend trying your usual size in the SCULPT SKINNY style.

Runs a bit small but I love it!! I LOVE this belt! I already purchased the other two colors, however I did size up in those as I find this runs a bit small. For reference, I am 5'7" and 135lbs with a 27-28" waist. I ordered the small and wearing them with high waisted jeans, it fits fine. There is enough slack where it doesn't look funny. But if I were to wear these with anything lower waisted, there would not be enough slack and it might look funny. I will update once I get the medium in the other colors how they fit. I have a belt hole punch if I need to add more holes. I do find it a bit tricky to get the belt buckled and unbuckled but its literally because of the shape of the buckle, not a flaw in the product. Once you get it buckled and laid flat, it looks great but getting it on at first, I was a bit worried! I noticed another reviewer said the gold was not a good color, but I like it. It doesn't look cheap to me. Overall, I really love this belt. Its a much more fun alternative to standard belts. It can easily be worn with jeans or with dress pants for the office. I would consider sizing up but if you are on the high end of the size range provided. Definitely recommend!!!

Serviceable but Don't Love It I feel that this runs a little large in comparison to the size chart. I just ordered my usual size instead, M, and overall the fit is ok and matches up to a typical M, I am just a bit too short & chubby for these to fit right. I am 5'3", 38-33-38 and the hem is about 3 inches too long, rise is too long for my taste (definitely a high waist on me when I would have preferred mid-rise). This is a very fine-boned corduroy fabric (see pic) and it does not stretch a lot apart from the elastic waist, so it's more like real pants than stretchy leggings. You may possibly want to order up and think of it more like baggy comfy pants? Or it may fit better on someone with a trim body. These won't fit well if you have large thighs (mine are 20" and I feel that they're about the max) or bootie, I think they will drag down on you. Overall these will probably work the best on someone with a 30-32" inseam & a slim-rectangle kind of body type. Looking at other reviewers that seems to match up with who was more happy with these pants. Really nice color selection (I was so tempted by both the greens but am surprisingly lacking in plain black pants) & they look good but they're a bit ill-fitting for me on comfort.

Pants for Fall Y’al Super comfy fall cords with elastic waist bands. Great for those nights going to friends house for football games and eating junk food and you still want to look cute. Also great pants for flying where you want to be relaxed but not look dumpy. I am 5’6” and ideally would have wanted another inch or so longer but will still be cute and even cuter with flats. They do run big and very big in the waist. I would not wear a tight shirt or short shirt with these bc they are so loose on my waist line. These can easily be dressed up or down which I appreciate. The reason I gave 4 stars is bc of the sizing on the waist line and length - but will definitely have lots of use for these fun fall pants.

Fun and comfortable These are comfortable pants with a good amount of stretch to them. My wife wasn’t sure what to expect with these pants but she is happy with them. The waist is elastic, but there are no belt loops and no front pockets. For reference she is 5’4 and about 120lbs and likes how the small fits her. They are a high waist fit and the length is just right, just barely touched the floor without shoes on. Overall she likes them. The color is a little more golden brown than khaki in person, but she still likes it and thinks they are versatile. The fit is flattering and comfortable. The quality could be better but is okay for the price but she thinks the front pockets and belt loops would add a lot to them. I hope this assists you with your shopping. Love these I'm 5'6, 132lbs, wear smalls/size 2(26). I ordered these pants in the small khaki color. The length is perfect for me but beware I'm more torso than I am legs. These are a lot of jean! I ordered these jeans as I like the style of them with the side pockets. Jeans can always be tricky to buy online but I've had some luck as of late so thought I would give these a try. The jeans are heavier than I was expecting. They are advertised as 'light' but I could tell just by the weight of the package that they were not as light as expected. I'm 5'4" and typically wear a size 12 so I ordered that same size. They fit fine in the waist and the hips but they are a bit long for me. I wish that the listing indicated the inseam vs. the total length because that's harder to judge on a high-waisted jean pair of jeans. I could still pull these off with the right platform boot or shoe so will wear them in those cases; especially since some jeans will shrink a bit in length with repeated washings. Otherwise, there is a little bit of stretch to them and they are reasonably comfortable.

Waist is a Little Too High and Length Could Be Longer for Me, But Still Very Wearable and Comfy I got a size 6 and the fit is pretty good. I typically go back and forth between size 4 and 6 on jeans depending on brand. Since I never owned this brand before, I took a shot at a 6. The fit is fine except the crotch depth and waist height is a little too deep for my figure, but that is typical on me with other brands where I get a size 6. All that said, I would guess the jeans are close to true to size.I am 5’8” and the jeans are long enough to wear, but only with flatter shoes. They fall to the back of my heal. I prefer a jean that almost touches the floor in the back, so these are about an inch to 1-1/2” shorter than I like. Once again, I find this problem with other brands so I don’t think this is a sizing issue, just a figure issue for me. (I’m kind of short waisted and long legged).The jeans are made well and I like the fabric. They feel like “broken in” jeans from the moment you put them on (a big plus for me). They feel nice on the skin. The fabric is also a nice weight - not too thin or too thick. I found them comfortable to wear. The waist stayed in place and they didn’t “bag” in any areas after a day of wearing. The color is perfect. I do like the straight leg cut of the jean. To me it’s much more comfortable than skinny jeans.I have never owned jeans with cargo pockets before, so this is something new. I have mixed feelings on the pockets. I think it’s just something I need to find the correct top to go with them and they will be fine.Overall, I think these are a great jean and run true to size. Five stars.

My search for the perfect cargo jeans is over! I have been struggling to find the perfect pair of cargo jeans until now...these ETELLO jeans are everything! The quality of the jeans are great! They are high waisted and the material is thick, but not stiff, as they do have a good bit of stretchiness to them! They are actually very comfortable and the fit is so flattering and perfect!! I think that these cargo jeans are not only on trend, but are super versatile and would look awesome with almost anything you pair them with, no matter what your style is! I am 5'0 and 116lbs and I ordered a size 6, which fits me so good. My recommend size was a 4, but I have never been able to fit into a size 4 jean, so I went with my usual size 6 and I am glad that I did. Yes, they are a bit long on me as you can see, but almost every pair of pants are since I am so short! Here are my measurements for reference: Waist: 28 inches Hips: 38 inches Thigh: 22 inches If you are wanting a pair of cargo jeans, then seriously look no further! I highly recommend these!!!

thick yet still comfy enough *pic* These jeans are somewhat thick but it's cotton-polyester-viscose-spandex material mix makes it softer & somewhat stretchy for its thickness. It can feel a little hot to wear during warm to hot weather though. The pants legs are also about an inch too long on my daughter's 5'5 ft pear-shaped figure (see pic). Hoping that washing this against its recommendations (i.e., cold wash, hang to dry) in warm washer water and drying them on high heat will cause them to shrink some in length to fit her better. Shrinking sideways would also enable its somewhat wider waistband fit her better without needing a belt. Got her the size 12 based on its Amazon size chart and is true to size. She pairs these wide leg jeans with a fitted Y2K top to complete her Y2K style. Looks good on her. Recommended.

Cute wide leg cargo jeans These ETTELO cargo jeans are so cute and on trend. I'm obsessed with high waist jeans now, so absolutely love that! Nice deep pockets that I can fit my hands into comfortably, too! Sizewise, I followed the chart and got a size 10. I have a 30" waist and 36" hips. Nice material; not sewn very well; fit a little off for me The material used to make this is soft & stretchy, so these are quite comfy due to that.There have been some missteps with the construction, however. The straps were threaded backward through the hardware, so the adjustable part is on the backside & the stitching is on top, so it looks bad. I’m going to have to rip them apart & resew. In addition, the belt loops on the back are uneven, with the one in the middle sewn higher up than the other two. There’s also some messy stitching by the front left pocket.The fit, for me, is a little off. I’m 5’8” 160lbs. These are a little shorter than I’d like, a little baggy up top & a little snug across my hips. These are common issues for me though. If I want something to be long enough, it ends up being too big in other areas. These will likely be relegated to my “outside work clothes” drawer, which is totally fine with me cuz I still want to feel cute when I’m doing yard work or painting my walls. Took off one star because of the construction issues.

Darling jeans, surprised at the price These are really flattering and comfortable - if it weren't for the lower quality button closure used in the front waistband and the need to wear these a bit to get the painted on lines to look natural, I would think these would be a lot more expensive. I wear a 28 in Mother Denim / Anthropologie and a size small fits great. I think if you are under a size 6 you would find the small too baggy. Keep in mind there is a LOT of stretch in these so you'll be comfy and should feel better about buying in S/M/L (I've always bought jeans in exact sizes so was surprised it would come in just those categories). The wide leg is very flattering and the large pockets in the back look great with a top. Will try to remember to snap a picture and upload. I told a friend they were from amazon and under $50 and she said, "no way." For reference I'm 5'7" 145lbs, wearing size small in "Lapis." The hem fits perfectly with flats for my height, might be a little long for the shorter girls but cuffed jeans are back in style.

I Think I'm in Love with it I'm a little in love with this pantsuit. I didn't want anything that looked like nurse's scrubs and I think I achieved my goal. I got lots of compliments. It fits really well. For reference, I am 5'8 and 170 lbs. I would recommend washing it before wearing it. I hand washed it in cold and a lot of black dye and chemicals came off. Then I line dried it. There were no wrinkles, but if you do get wrinkles i would say not to iron it because it is a rather sheer, thin fabric. It's comfy, and you can dress it up or down. I balked a little at the price, but it's really worth it.

Green One Piece Plus Size Jump Suit The Green All-in-One Plus Size arrived sooner than expected with Amazon Prime.This Plus Size one piece runs small in the tummy, hips and thigh areas. It is designed for someone with a long turso and height over 5'4". The sleeves and legs of the one piece are long, if you have short arms and legs, it gathers well.It is not designed for anyone owning a foopah. It is not designed for that shape.The one piece is beautiful, the color is as pictured and it is not sheer, so there is no see through the material. However, there is no give in the outfit and to be comfortable for sitting and removing if you have to go to the bathroom requires you to size up 1 size for comfort and flexibility. I purchased a 2x, I am between 221 and 241. I am about 5'2". It does not flatter my pear shape, petite body type, however, I will try to make it work because it is very pretty.Wore it Today at Church for Palm Sunday. It was a HIT. Folks actually touched it to feel the material, it is gorgeous. I will be passing this jumpsuit on to someone who has the height and shape for it. Really nice jumpsuit, runs slim in the torso, stomach and hips area. However, it is rather flattering on the body. I did not wear the belt, I could not find it after having this in the closet for so long. The belt was not missed.

Not realistic model photos 😔 These are a dupe of EB Denim’s Fredrick Jean. I was super excited to find these since the original retails for 400$. However, the photos on Amazon are of the original, not the dupe. These are a light weight denim in a light wash with faded spots on the front and back of thighs that are almost white (and not flattering). The 3 seams across the knees are so cute. There is a fit issue for me - too much fabric in the front crotch area. It bunches up weird. I’m 5’4 and these just skim the ground, perfect for wearing flats or low heels. I’m usually a 14/16 in pants and the XL fits. However, I’m used to stretch denim and these are non-stretch. They do have an elastic back to the waist band. I find them a little uncomfortable in the front waist area. They are more straight legged than barrel jean. I think these are a return for me.

Bell Bottom Jeans I am 5'4 175lbs and normally wear 10 or 12 pants. Looking at other reviews I ordered an XL. It was a little big in the waist and may have been okay with a large, but I secure it with a belt and pulled it up as it is a high-waisted style. I needed it for a 70's theme holiday party and it work out great. Another person had on the same pants but it looked different on them because they were much taller than me so the bottom didn't look as belled as mine. Because of my height, I kept tripping on my pants legs when I walked upstairs. I had on wedge heel sneakers but it wasn't high enough to avoid dragging on the ground. I notice on the left leg the outer seam kept moving to the middle of my legs and I had to keep adjusting the leg. Other than that, I will wear them again.

Ruffles and thick These jeans are cute but they are pretty bulky. If you are a bit wider in the hip area they will make you look even wider. These would be great for winter but in Florida they are a little too thick for April weather. They are fitted in the hips (curvy girl). I think they will add curves to someone who is more of a rectangular shape and be quite flattering. If you have a small waist and hips you may not want the extra baggage, which is why I’m torn about keeping them or getting a refund. The front of the jeans don’t have any tummy support so I would not wear them when bloated. The button isn’t as secure as mainstream brand jeans, either. For reference I’m 5’4 and a half inches tall with a short torso. If I decide to keep them, I will need to cut the stings that are hanging off of the ruffles.

Definitely more of a low rise (perfect) a bit of a flair but not crazy, just enough to go over boots/shoes. Stretchy but not form fitting, comfortable. I’m 5’5 and they are a bit long even with a wedge but not enough that it takes any stars away!
